content == {{神社 |名称 = 養父神社 |画像 = [[File:Yabu-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|養父市|Q869014}}養父市場840 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|14.6|N|134|48|13.7|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|ウカノミタマ|Q3080728|倉稲魂命}}
{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大己貴命}}
{{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|少彦名命}}
{{jalink|丹波道主王||谿羽道主命}}
船帆足尼命 |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}2座、小3座）
{{jalink|但馬国|Q1053727}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|三宮}}
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}30年 |本殿 = {{jalink|入母屋造|Q855025}}{{jalink|檜皮葺|Q90373477}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} |神事 = お走り祭り（4月第3日曜日とその前日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=養父神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yabu-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|境内入り口}}]] '''養父神社'''（やぶじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|養父市|Q869014}}養父市場にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|但馬国|Q1053727}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|三宮}}、{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}2座、小社3座）で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|県社}}。 「養父の明神さん」と呼ばれ、農業の神として知られる。養父市場は古くから{{jalink|但馬牛|Q13417172}}の牛市の中心地であり、現在でも近隣の大藪で但馬牛のせり市が開かれている。 === 祭神 === 現在の祭神は次の5座。 * {{jalink|ウカノミタマ|Q3080728|倉稲魂命}} * {{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大己貴命}} * {{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|少彦名命}} * {{jalink|丹波道主王||谿羽道主命}} * 船帆足尼命 - 谿羽道主命の子孫で但馬国造の祖 江戸時代に編纂された地誌『但馬考』にはかつて弥高山の山頂にあったとされる上社に大己貴命、中腹の中社に倉稲魂命と少彦名命、現社地である下社に谿羽道主命を祀るとの記述がある。『{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}』では大己貴命以外の4座を不詳としている。1928年（昭和3年）発行の『養父郡誌』では上社が{{jalink|保食神|Q2280323}}と{{jalink|五十猛神|Q10881556}}、中社が少彦名命、下社が谿羽道主命と船帆足尼命としている『日本の神々(7)山陰』331頁。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}30年創祀と伝えられ、{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}9年（{{jalink|737年|Q23583}}）の『但馬国税正帳』にも{{jalink|出石神社|Q3156511}}、{{jalink|粟鹿神社|Q11604651}}とともにその名が見える。 神階は{{jalink|承和 (日本)|Q1075605|承和}}12年（845年）に従五位下を授けられ（『{{jalink|続日本後紀|Q1041301}}』）、貞観11年（869年）に正五位下、同16年（874年）に正五位上まで昇叙した（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』には「夜夫坐神社五座（名神大二座小三座）」との記載がある。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}には水谷山普賢寺が別当寺であった。 {{jalink|1814年|Q6943}}（文化11年）には、{{jalink|伊能忠敬|Q1240937}}が{{jalink|測量|Q816425}}に訪れている。 === 境内 === File:Yabu-jinja, shaden-2.jpg|社殿 File:Yabu-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿 File:Yabu-jinja, tou.jpg|宝篋印塔（養父市指定文化財） File:Yabu-jinja, Yamanokuchi-jinja.jpg|山野口神社 File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|境内社 File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|境内社 File:Yabu-jinja, torii.jpg|鳥居 === 祭事 === * {{jalink|2月25日|Q2356}} 春季大祭（{{jalink|祈年祭|Q118906710}}） * 4月第3日曜日とその前日 {{jalink|お走りさん|Q11263474|お走り祭り}} - 養父市指定無形民俗文化財。 * {{jalink|5月2日|Q2545}} {{jalink|八十八夜|Q11390609}}祭 * {{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} 秋季大祭（{{jalink|例祭|Q11385469}}） === 文化財 === === 兵庫県登録文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 養父神社 5棟（建造物） - 2010年（平成22年）3月5日登録{{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/29kentouroku.pdf |title=県登録文化財一覧 |website=兵庫県立教育研修所 |accessdate=2020-01-13}}。 *** 本殿 - 江戸時代末期の造営。 *** 拝殿 - 江戸時代末期の造営。 *** 摂社山野口神社本殿 - 江戸時代末期の造営。 *** 摂社五社神社本殿 - 江戸時代末期の造営。 === 養父市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 石造宝篋印塔 - 1977年（昭和52年）5月18日指定石造宝篋印塔 説明板。。 * 無形民俗文化財 ** お走り祭り === 交通 === * 鉄道・バス ** JR{{jalink|山陰本線|Q1135170}}{{jalink|養父駅|Q871175}}より徒歩約2km ** JR山陰本線養父駅より{{jalink|全但バス|Q11389110}}「八鹿駅」行きで養父明神下車すぐ ** JR山陰本線{{jalink|八鹿駅|Q870988}}より全但バス「和田山・山口」行きで養父明神下車すぐ * 道路 ** {{jalink|舞鶴若狭自動車道|Q11613730}}・{{jalink|福知山インターチェンジ|Q11594317|福知山IC}}より約1時間 ** {{jalink|遠阪トンネル有料道路}}・朝来市側出口より約20分 ** {{jalink|播但連絡道路|Q11498699}}・{{jalink|和田山インターチェンジ|Q10921611|和田山IC}}より約15分 ** {{jalink|道の駅やぶ|Q11640978}}および{{jalink|道の駅但馬楽座|Q11641146}}より約5分 ===周辺=== * {{jalink|大藪古墳群|Q83458374}} * ほたるの里 * {{jalink|満福寺 (養父市)|Q43594125|満福寺}} - 「但馬の高野山」と呼ばれる{{jalink|名刹}}。 === 脚注 === {{reflist}} === 参考文献 == = *{{jalink|谷川健一|Q2392596}}編 『日本の神々 神社と聖地 7 山陰』（新装復刊） 2000年 {{jalink|白水社|Q4346470}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 *『式内社調査報告』第19巻 山陰道2、皇學館大學出版部、1984年（昭和59年） *『兵庫縣神社誌』下巻（兵庫縣神職會刊行、1938年〈昭和13年〉の復刻）、臨川書店、1984年（昭和59年） ISBN 4-653-01102-8 *[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1175348 『丹後史料叢書』第4輯]、丹後史料叢書刊行会、1927年（昭和2年） 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション（参照 2025年6月17日） ===外部リンク=== {{Commonscat|Yabu-jinja}} * [http://www.yabu-jinja.jp/ 養父神社] - 公式サイト * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/380201.html 夜夫坐神社五座] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:養父市の建築物]] [[Category:養父市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:但馬国の式内社|名やふしんしや]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:但馬国|社3]] [[Category:need translation]]

船帆足尼命 |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}2座、小3座）
{{jalink|但馬国|Q1053727}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|三宮}}
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}30年 |本殿 = {{jalink|入母屋造|Q855025}}{{jalink|檜皮葺|Q90373477}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} |神事 = お走り祭り（4月第3日曜日とその前日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=養父神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yabu-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|境内入り口}}]] '''養父神社'''（やぶじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|養父市|Q869014}}養父市場にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|但馬国|Q1053727}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|三宮}}、{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}2座、小社3座）で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|県社}}。 「養父の明神さん」と呼ばれ、農業の神として知られる。養父市場は古くから{{jalink|但馬牛|Q13417172}}の牛市の中心地であり、現在でも近隣の大藪で但馬牛のせり市が開かれている。 === 祭神 === 現在の祭神は次の5座。 * {{jalink|ウカノミタマ|Q3080728|倉稲魂命}} * {{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大己貴命}} * {{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|少彦名命}} * {{jalink|丹波道主王||谿羽道主命}} * 船帆足尼命 - 谿羽道主命の子孫で但馬国造の祖 江戸時代に編纂された地誌『但馬考』にはかつて弥高山の山頂にあったとされる上社に大己貴命、中腹の中社に倉稲魂命と少彦名命、現社地である下社に谿羽道主命を祀るとの記述がある。『{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}』では大己貴命以外の4座を不詳としている。1928年（昭和3年）発行の『養父郡誌』では上社が{{jalink|保食神|Q2280323}}と{{jalink|五十猛神|Q10881556}}、中社が少彦名命、下社が谿羽道主命と船帆足尼命としている『日本の神々(7)山陰』331頁。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}30年創祀と伝えられ、{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}9年（{{jalink|737年|Q23583}}）の『但馬国税正帳』にも{{jalink|出石神社|Q3156511}}、{{jalink|粟鹿神社|Q11604651}}とともにその名が見える。 神階は{{jalink|承和 (日本)|Q1075605|承和}}12年（845年）に従五位下を授けられ（『{{jalink|続日本後紀|Q1041301}}』）、貞観11年（869年）に正五位下、同16年（874年）に正五位上まで昇叙した（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』には「夜夫坐神社五座（名神大二座小三座）」との記載がある。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}には水谷山普賢寺が別当寺であった。 {{jalink|1814年|Q6943}}（文化11年）には、{{jalink|伊能忠敬|Q1240937}}が{{jalink|測量|Q816425}}に訪れている。 === 境内 === File:Yabu-jinja, shaden-2.jpg|社殿 File:Yabu-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿 File:Yabu-jinja, tou.jpg|宝篋印塔（養父市指定文化財） File:Yabu-jinja, Yamanokuchi-jinja.jpg|山野口神社 File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|境内社 File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|境内社 File:Yabu-jinja, torii.jpg|鳥居 === 祭事 === * {{jalink|2月25日|Q2356}} 春季大祭（{{jalink|祈年祭|Q118906710}}） * 4月第3日曜日とその前日 {{jalink|お走りさん|Q11263474|お走り祭り}} - 養父市指定無形民俗文化財。 * {{jalink|5月2日|Q2545}} {{jalink|八十八夜|Q11390609}}祭 * {{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} 秋季大祭（{{jalink|例祭|Q11385469}}） === 文化財 === === 兵庫県登録文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 養父神社 5棟（建造物） - 2010年（平成22年）3月5日登録{{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/29kentouroku.pdf |title=県登録文化財一覧 |website=兵庫県立教育研修所 |accessdate=2020-01-13}}。 *** 本殿 - 江戸時代末期の造営。 *** 拝殿 - 江戸時代末期の造営。 *** 摂社山野口神社本殿 - 江戸時代末期の造営。 *** 摂社五社神社本殿 - 江戸時代末期の造営。 === 養父市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 石造宝篋印塔 - 1977年（昭和52年）5月18日指定石造宝篋印塔 説明板。。 * 無形民俗文化財 ** お走り祭り === 交通 === * 鉄道・バス ** JR{{jalink|山陰本線|Q1135170}}{{jalink|養父駅|Q871175}}より徒歩約2km ** JR山陰本線養父駅より{{jalink|全但バス|Q11389110}}「八鹿駅」行きで養父明神下車すぐ ** JR山陰本線{{jalink|八鹿駅|Q870988}}より全但バス「和田山・山口」行きで養父明神下車すぐ * 道路 ** {{jalink|舞鶴若狭自動車道|Q11613730}}・{{jalink|福知山インターチェンジ|Q11594317|福知山IC}}より約1時間 ** {{jalink|遠阪トンネル有料道路}}・朝来市側出口より約20分 ** {{jalink|播但連絡道路|Q11498699}}・{{jalink|和田山インターチェンジ|Q10921611|和田山IC}}より約15分 ** {{jalink|道の駅やぶ|Q11640978}}および{{jalink|道の駅但馬楽座|Q11641146}}より約5分 ===周辺=== * {{jalink|大藪古墳群|Q83458374}} * ほたるの里 * {{jalink|満福寺 (養父市)|Q43594125|満福寺}} - 「但馬の高野山」と呼ばれる{{jalink|名刹}}。 === 脚注 === {{reflist}} === 参考文献 == = *{{jalink|谷川健一|Q2392596}}編 『日本の神々 神社と聖地 7 山陰』（新装復刊） 2000年 {{jalink|白水社|Q4346470}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 *『式内社調査報告』第19巻 山陰道2、皇學館大學出版部、1984年（昭和59年） *『兵庫縣神社誌』下巻（兵庫縣神職會刊行、1938年〈昭和13年〉の復刻）、臨川書店、1984年（昭和59年） ISBN 4-653-01102-8 *[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1175348 『丹後史料叢書』第4輯]、丹後史料叢書刊行会、1927年（昭和2年） 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション（参照 2025年6月17日） ===外部リンク=== {{Commonscat|Yabu-jinja}} * [http://www.yabu-jinja.jp/ 養父神社] - 公式サイト * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/380201.html 夜夫坐神社五座] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:養父市の建築物]] [[Category:養父市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:但馬国の式内社|名やふしんしや]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:但馬国|社3]] [[Category:need translation]]

谿羽道主命の子孫で但馬国造の祖 江戸時代に編纂された地誌『但馬考』にはかつて弥高山の山頂にあったとされる上社に大己貴命、中腹の中社に倉稲魂命と少彦名命、現社地である下社に谿羽道主命を祀るとの記述がある。『{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}』では大己貴命以外の4座を不詳としている。1928年（昭和3年）発行の『養父郡誌』では上社が{{jalink|保食神|Q2280323}}と{{jalink|五十猛神|Q10881556}}、中社が少彦名命、下社が谿羽道主命と船帆足尼命としている『日本の神々(7)山陰』331頁。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}30年創祀と伝えられ、{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}9年（{{jalink|737年|Q23583}}）の『但馬国税正帳』にも{{jalink|出石神社|Q3156511}}、{{jalink|粟鹿神社|Q11604651}}とともにその名が見える。 神階は{{jalink|承和 (日本)|Q1075605|承和}}12年（845年）に従五位下を授けられ（『{{jalink|続日本後紀|Q1041301}}』）、貞観11年（869年）に正五位下、同16年（874年）に正五位上まで昇叙した（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』には「夜夫坐神社五座（名神大二座小三座）」との記載がある。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}には水谷山普賢寺が別当寺であった。 {{jalink|1814年|Q6943}}（文化11年）には、{{jalink|伊能忠敬|Q1240937}}が{{jalink|測量|Q816425}}に訪れている。 === 境内 === File:Yabu-jinja, shaden-2.jpg|社殿 File:Yabu-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿 File:Yabu-jinja, tou.jpg|宝篋印塔（養父市指定文化財） File:Yabu-jinja, Yamanokuchi-jinja.jpg|山野口神社 File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|境内社 File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|境内社 File:Yabu-jinja, torii.jpg|鳥居 === 祭事 === * {{jalink|2月25日|Q2356}} 春季大祭（{{jalink|祈年祭|Q118906710}}） * 4月第3日曜日とその前日 {{jalink|お走りさん|Q11263474|お走り祭り}} - 養父市指定無形民俗文化財。 * {{jalink|5月2日|Q2545}} {{jalink|八十八夜|Q11390609}}祭 * {{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} 秋季大祭（{{jalink|例祭|Q11385469}}） === 文化財 === === 兵庫県登録文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 養父神社 5棟（建造物） - 2010年（平成22年）3月5日登録{{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/29kentouroku.pdf |title=県登録文化財一覧 |website=兵庫県立教育研修所 |accessdate=2020-01-13}}。 *** 本殿 - 江戸時代末期の造営。 *** 拝殿 - 江戸時代末期の造営。 *** 摂社山野口神社本殿 - 江戸時代末期の造営。 *** 摂社五社神社本殿 - 江戸時代末期の造営。 === 養父市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 石造宝篋印塔 - 1977年（昭和52年）5月18日指定石造宝篋印塔 説明板。。 * 無形民俗文化財 ** お走り祭り === 交通 === * 鉄道・バス ** JR{{jalink|山陰本線|Q1135170}}{{jalink|養父駅|Q871175}}より徒歩約2km ** JR山陰本線養父駅より{{jalink|全但バス|Q11389110}}「八鹿駅」行きで養父明神下車すぐ ** JR山陰本線{{jalink|八鹿駅|Q870988}}より全但バス「和田山・山口」行きで養父明神下車すぐ * 道路 ** {{jalink|舞鶴若狭自動車道|Q11613730}}・{{jalink|福知山インターチェンジ|Q11594317|福知山IC}}より約1時間 ** {{jalink|遠阪トンネル有料道路}}・朝来市側出口より約20分 ** {{jalink|播但連絡道路|Q11498699}}・{{jalink|和田山インターチェンジ|Q10921611|和田山IC}}より約15分 ** {{jalink|道の駅やぶ|Q11640978}}および{{jalink|道の駅但馬楽座|Q11641146}}より約5分 ===周辺=== * {{jalink|大藪古墳群|Q83458374}} * ほたるの里 * {{jalink|満福寺 (養父市)|Q43594125|満福寺}} - 「但馬の高野山」と呼ばれる{{jalink|名刹}}。 === 脚注 === {{reflist}} === 参考文献 == = *{{jalink|谷川健一|Q2392596}}編 『日本の神々 神社と聖地 7 山陰』（新装復刊） 2000年 {{jalink|白水社|Q4346470}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 *『式内社調査報告』第19巻 山陰道2、皇學館大學出版部、1984年（昭和59年） *『兵庫縣神社誌』下巻（兵庫縣神職會刊行、1938年〈昭和13年〉の復刻）、臨川書店、1984年（昭和59年） ISBN 4-653-01102-8 *[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1175348 『丹後史料叢書』第4輯]、丹後史料叢書刊行会、1927年（昭和2年） 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション（参照 2025年6月17日） ===外部リンク=== {{Commonscat|Yabu-jinja}} * [http://www.yabu-jinja.jp/ 養父神社] - 公式サイト * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/380201.html 夜夫坐神社五座] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:養父市の建築物]] [[Category:養父市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:但馬国の式内社|名やふしんしや]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:但馬国|社3]] [[Category:need translation]]

谿羽道主命の子孫で但馬国造の祖 江戸時代に編纂された地誌『但馬考』にはかつて弥高山の山頂にあったとされる上社に大己貴命、中腹の中社に倉稲魂命と少彦名命、現社地である下社に谿羽道主命を祀るとの記述がある。『{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}』では大己貴命以外の4座を不詳としている。1928年（昭和3年）発行の『養父郡誌』では上社が{{jalink|保食神|Q2280323}}と{{jalink|五十猛神|Q10881556}}、中社が少彦名命、下社が谿羽道主命と船帆足尼命としている『日本の神々(7)山陰』331頁。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}30年創祀と伝えられ、{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}9年（{{jalink|737年|Q23583}}）の『但馬国税正帳』にも{{jalink|出石神社|Q3156511}}、{{jalink|粟鹿神社|Q11604651}}とともにその名が見える。 神階は{{jalink|承和 (日本)|Q1075605|承和}}12年（845年）に従五位下を授けられ（『{{jalink|続日本後紀|Q1041301}}』）、貞観11年（869年）に正五位下、同16年（874年）に正五位上まで昇叙した（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』には「夜夫坐神社五座（名神大二座小三座）」との記載がある。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}には水谷山普賢寺が別当寺であった。 {{jalink|1814年|Q6943}}（文化11年）には、{{jalink|伊能忠敬|Q1240937}}が{{jalink|測量|Q816425}}に訪れている。 === 境内 === File:Yabu-jinja, shaden-2.jpg|社殿 File:Yabu-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿 File:Yabu-jinja, tou.jpg|宝篋印塔（養父市指定文化財） File:Yabu-jinja, Yamanokuchi-jinja.jpg|山野口神社 File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|境内社 File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|境内社 File:Yabu-jinja, torii.jpg|鳥居 === 祭事 === * {{jalink|2月25日|Q2356}} 春季大祭（{{jalink|祈年祭|Q118906710}}） * 4月第3日曜日とその前日 {{jalink|お走りさん|Q11263474|お走り祭り}} - 養父市指定無形民俗文化財。 * {{jalink|5月2日|Q2545}} {{jalink|八十八夜|Q11390609}}祭 * {{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} 秋季大祭（{{jalink|例祭|Q11385469}}） === 文化財 === === 兵庫県登録文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 養父神社 5棟（建造物） - 2010年（平成22年）3月5日登録{{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/29kentouroku.pdf |title=県登録文化財一覧 |website=兵庫県立教育研修所 |accessdate=2020-01-13}}。 *** 本殿 - 江戸時代末期の造営。 *** 拝殿 - 江戸時代末期の造営。 *** 摂社山野口神社本殿 - 江戸時代末期の造営。 *** 摂社五社神社本殿 - 江戸時代末期の造営。 === 養父市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 石造宝篋印塔 - 1977年（昭和52年）5月18日指定石造宝篋印塔 説明板。。 * 無形民俗文化財 ** お走り祭り === 交通 === * 鉄道・バス ** JR{{jalink|山陰本線|Q1135170}}{{jalink|養父駅|Q871175}}より徒歩約2km ** JR山陰本線養父駅より{{jalink|全但バス|Q11389110}}「八鹿駅」行きで養父明神下車すぐ ** JR山陰本線{{jalink|八鹿駅|Q870988}}より全但バス「和田山・山口」行きで養父明神下車すぐ * 道路 ** {{jalink|舞鶴若狭自動車道|Q11613730}}・{{jalink|福知山インターチェンジ|Q11594317|福知山IC}}より約1時間 ** {{jalink|遠阪トンネル有料道路}}・朝来市側出口より約20分 ** {{jalink|播但連絡道路|Q11498699}}・{{jalink|和田山インターチェンジ|Q10921611|和田山IC}}より約15分 ** {{jalink|道の駅やぶ|Q11640978}}および{{jalink|道の駅但馬楽座|Q11641146}}より約5分 ===周辺=== * {{jalink|大藪古墳群|Q83458374}} * ほたるの里 * {{jalink|満福寺 (養父市)|Q43594125|満福寺}} - 「但馬の高野山」と呼ばれる{{jalink|名刹}}。 === 脚注 === {{reflist}} === 参考文献 == = *{{jalink|谷川健一|Q2392596}}編 『日本の神々 神社と聖地 7 山陰』（新装復刊） 2000年 {{jalink|白水社|Q4346470}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 *『式内社調査報告』第19巻 山陰道2、皇學館大學出版部、1984年（昭和59年） *『兵庫縣神社誌』下巻（兵庫縣神職會刊行、1938年〈昭和13年〉の復刻）、臨川書店、1984年（昭和59年） ISBN 4-653-01102-8 *[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1175348 『丹後史料叢書』第4輯]、丹後史料叢書刊行会、1927年（昭和2年） 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション（参照 2025年6月17日） ===外部リンク=== {{Commonscat|Yabu-jinja}} * [http://www.yabu-jinja.jp/ 養父神社] - 公式サイト * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/380201.html 夜夫坐神社五座] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:養父市の建築物]] [[Category:養父市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:但馬国の式内社|名やふしんしや]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:但馬国|社3]] [[Category:need translation]]

旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}30年 |本殿 = {{jalink|入母屋造|Q855025}}{{jalink|檜皮葺|Q90373477}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} |神事 = お走り祭り（4月第3日曜日とその前日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=養父神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yabu-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|境内入り口}}]] '''養父神社'''（やぶじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|養父市|Q869014}}養父市場にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|但馬国|Q1053727}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|三宮}}、{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}2座、小社3座）で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|県社}}。 「養父の明神さん」と呼ばれ、農業の神として知られる。養父市場は古くから{{jalink|但馬牛|Q13417172}}の牛市の中心地であり、現在でも近隣の大藪で但馬牛のせり市が開かれている。 === 祭神 === 現在の祭神は次の5座。 * {{jalink|ウカノミタマ|Q3080728|倉稲魂命}} * {{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大己貴命}} * {{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|少彦名命}} * {{jalink|丹波道主王||谿羽道主命}} * 船帆足尼命 - 谿羽道主命の子孫で但馬国造の祖 江戸時代に編纂された地誌『但馬考』にはかつて弥高山の山頂にあったとされる上社に大己貴命、中腹の中社に倉稲魂命と少彦名命、現社地である下社に谿羽道主命を祀るとの記述がある。『{{jalink|特選神名牒|Q11571092}}』では大己貴命以外の4座を不詳としている。1928年（昭和3年）発行の『養父郡誌』では上社が{{jalink|保食神|Q2280323}}と{{jalink|五十猛神|Q10881556}}、中社が少彦名命、下社が谿羽道主命と船帆足尼命としている『日本の神々(7)山陰』331頁。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}30年創祀と伝えられ、{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}9年（{{jalink|737年|Q23583}}）の『但馬国税正帳』にも{{jalink|出石神社|Q3156511}}、{{jalink|粟鹿神社|Q11604651}}とともにその名が見える。 神階は{{jalink|承和 (日本)|Q1075605|承和}}12年（845年）に従五位下を授けられ（『{{jalink|続日本後紀|Q1041301}}』）、貞観11年（869年）に正五位下、同16年（874年）に正五位上まで昇叙した（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』には「夜夫坐神社五座（名神大二座小三座）」との記載がある。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}には水谷山普賢寺が別当寺であった。 {{jalink|1814年|Q6943}}（文化11年）には、{{jalink|伊能忠敬|Q1240937}}が{{jalink|測量|Q816425}}に訪れている。 === 境内 === File:Yabu-jinja, shaden-2.jpg|社殿 File:Yabu-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿 File:Yabu-jinja, tou.jpg|宝篋印塔（養父市指定文化財） File:Yabu-jinja, Yamanokuchi-jinja.jpg|山野口神社 File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|境内社 File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|境内社 File:Yabu-jinja, torii.jpg|鳥居 === 祭事 === * {{jalink|2月25日|Q2356}} 春季大祭（{{jalink|祈年祭|Q118906710}}） * 4月第3日曜日とその前日 {{jalink|お走りさん|Q11263474|お走り祭り}} - 養父市指定無形民俗文化財。 * {{jalink|5月2日|Q2545}} {{jalink|八十八夜|Q11390609}}祭 * {{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} 秋季大祭（{{jalink|例祭|Q11385469}}） === 文化財 === === 兵庫県登録文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 養父神社 5棟（建造物） - 2010年（平成22年）3月5日登録{{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/29kentouroku.pdf |title=県登録文化財一覧 |website=兵庫県立教育研修所 |accessdate=2020-01-13}}。 *** 本殿 - 江戸時代末期の造営。 *** 拝殿 - 江戸時代末期の造営。 *** 摂社山野口神社本殿 - 江戸時代末期の造営。 *** 摂社五社神社本殿 - 江戸時代末期の造営。 === 養父市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 石造宝篋印塔 - 1977年（昭和52年）5月18日指定石造宝篋印塔 説明板。。 * 無形民俗文化財 ** お走り祭り === 交通 === * 鉄道・バス ** JR{{jalink|山陰本線|Q1135170}}{{jalink|養父駅|Q871175}}より徒歩約2km ** JR山陰本線養父駅より{{jalink|全但バス|Q11389110}}「八鹿駅」行きで養父明神下車すぐ ** JR山陰本線{{jalink|八鹿駅|Q870988}}より全但バス「和田山・山口」行きで養父明神下車すぐ * 道路 ** {{jalink|舞鶴若狭自動車道|Q11613730}}・{{jalink|福知山インターチェンジ|Q11594317|福知山IC}}より約1時間 ** {{jalink|遠阪トンネル有料道路}}・朝来市側出口より約20分 ** {{jalink|播但連絡道路|Q11498699}}・{{jalink|和田山インターチェンジ|Q10921611|和田山IC}}より約15分 ** {{jalink|道の駅やぶ|Q11640978}}および{{jalink|道の駅但馬楽座|Q11641146}}より約5分 ===周辺=== * {{jalink|大藪古墳群|Q83458374}} * ほたるの里 * {{jalink|満福寺 (養父市)|Q43594125|満福寺}} - 「但馬の高野山」と呼ばれる{{jalink|名刹}}。 === 脚注 === {{reflist}} === 参考文献 == = *{{jalink|谷川健一|Q2392596}}編 『日本の神々 神社と聖地 7 山陰』（新装復刊） 2000年 {{jalink|白水社|Q4346470}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 *『式内社調査報告』第19巻 山陰道2、皇學館大學出版部、1984年（昭和59年） *『兵庫縣神社誌』下巻（兵庫縣神職會刊行、1938年〈昭和13年〉の復刻）、臨川書店、1984年（昭和59年） ISBN 4-653-01102-8 *[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1175348 『丹後史料叢書』第4輯]、丹後史料叢書刊行会、1927年（昭和2年） 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション（参照 2025年6月17日） ===外部リンク=== {{Commonscat|Yabu-jinja}} * [http://www.yabu-jinja.jp/ 養父神社] - 公式サイト * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/380201.html 夜夫坐神社五座] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:養父市の建築物]] [[Category:養父市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:但馬国の式内社|名やふしんしや]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:但馬国|社3]] [[Category:need translation]]

content == {{神社 |名称 = Yabu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yabu-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{jalink|Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Yabu|Q869014}} Ichiba 840, Yabu City |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|14.6|N|134|48|13.7|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Tanba Michinushi no Kimi||Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}}
Funahotari-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|myōjin-taisha}}: 2 seats, small: 3 seats)
{{jalink|Tajima Province|Q1053727}} {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379|Sannomiya}}
former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = (tradition) 30th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} |本殿 = {{jalink|irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} with {{jalink|hinoki bark roofing|Q90373477}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |神事 = Oshiri Festival (third Sunday of April and the preceding day) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Yabu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yabu-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Precinct entrance}}]] '''Yabu Shrine''' (やぶじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Ichiba, {{jalink|Yabu|Q869014}}, {{jalink|Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290}}. It is the {{jalink|Tajima Province|Q1053727}} {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379|third-ranking provincial shrine (sannomiya)}}, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (2 myōjin-taisha seats and 3 small-shrine seats), and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|kensha}}. It is popularly called "Yabu no Myojin-san" and is known as a deity of agriculture. Ichiba in Yabu has long been a center of cattle markets for {{jalink|Tajima cattle|Q13417172}}, and auctions are still held at nearby Ōyabu. === Enshrined deities === The five currently enshrined seats are: * {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Tanba Michinushi no Kimi||Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}} * Funahotari-no-Mikoto - descendant of Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and ancestor of the provincial rulers of Tajima In ''Tajima-kō'', a regional record compiled in the Edo period, it is described that once the upper shrine on Mt. Yadaka enshrined Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto, the middle shrine on the mid-slope enshrined Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto and Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine at the present site enshrined Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto. ''{{jalink|Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092}}'' treats four seats other than Ōanamuji as uncertain. ''Yabu Gunshi'' (1928) gives the upper shrine deities as {{jalink|Ukemochi|Q2280323}} and {{jalink|Isotakeru|Q10881556}}, the middle shrine as Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine as Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and Funahotari-no-Mikoto''Gods of Japan (7) San'in'', p.331. === History === Tradition says the shrine was founded in the 30th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, and it is already named together with {{jalink|Izushi Shrine|Q3156511}} and {{jalink|Awaga Shrine|Q11604651}} in the ''Tajima no Kuni Zeiseichō'' of {{jalink|Tenpyō|Q1199879}} 9 ({{jalink|737|Q23583}}). For divine court rank, it received junior fifth rank lower in {{jalink|Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 12 (845) (''{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301}}''), rose to senior fifth rank lower in Jōgan 11 (869), and to senior fifth rank upper in Jōgan 16 (874) (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}''). The ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' records "Yabufuza Shrine, five seats (two myōjin-tai and three small seats)". In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, Suisenzan Fugen-ji served as the attached temple. In {{jalink|1814|Q6943}} (Bunka 11), {{jalink|Ino Tadataka|Q1240937}} visited during his {{jalink|surveying|Q816425}} expedition. === Precinct === File:Yabu-jinja, shaden-2.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Yabu-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall File:Yabu-jinja, tou.jpg|Stone hōkyōintō pagoda (Yabu City designated cultural property) File:Yabu-jinja, Yamanokuchi-jinja.jpg|Yamanokuchi Shrine File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Festivals === * {{jalink|February 25|Q2356}} Spring Grand Festival ({{jalink|Kinen-sai|Q118906710}}) * Third Sunday of April and preceding day: {{jalink|Ohashiri-san|Q11263474|Oshiri Festival}} - designated intangible folk cultural property of Yabu City * {{jalink|May 2|Q2545}} {{jalink|Hachijūhachiya|Q11390609}} Festival * {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} Autumn Grand Festival ({{jalink|annual festival|Q11385469}}) === Cultural properties === === Registered cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture === * Tangible cultural properties ** Yabu Shrine, 5 structures (buildings) - registered March 5, 2010 (Heisei 22){{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/29kentouroku.pdf |title=List of prefecturally registered cultural properties |website=Hyogo Prefectural Education and Training Institute |accessdate=2020-01-13}}. *** Main hall - content == {{神社 |名称 = Yabu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yabu-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{jalink|Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Yabu|Q869014}} Ichiba 840, Yabu City |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|14.6|N|134|48|13.7|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Tanba Michinushi no Kimi||Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}}
Funahotari-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|myōjin-taisha}}: 2 seats, small: 3 seats)
{{jalink|Tajima Province|Q1053727}} {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379|Sannomiya}}
former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = (tradition) 30th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} |本殿 = {{jalink|irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} with {{jalink|hinoki bark roofing|Q90373477}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |神事 = Oshiri Festival (third Sunday of April and the preceding day) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Yabu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yabu-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Precinct entrance}}]] '''Yabu Shrine''' (やぶじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Ichiba, {{jalink|Yabu|Q869014}}, {{jalink|Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290}}. It is the {{jalink|Tajima Province|Q1053727}} {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379|third-ranking provincial shrine (sannomiya)}}, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (2 myōjin-taisha seats and 3 small-shrine seats), and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|kensha}}. It is popularly called "Yabu no Myojin-san" and is known as a deity of agriculture. Ichiba in Yabu has long been a center of cattle markets for {{jalink|Tajima cattle|Q13417172}}, and auctions are still held at nearby Ōyabu. === Enshrined deities === The five currently enshrined seats are: * {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Tanba Michinushi no Kimi||Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}} * Funahotari-no-Mikoto - descendant of Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and ancestor of the provincial rulers of Tajima In ''Tajima-kō'', a regional record compiled in the Edo period, it is described that once the upper shrine on Mt. Yadaka enshrined Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto, the middle shrine on the mid-slope enshrined Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto and Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine at the present site enshrined Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto. ''{{jalink|Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092}}'' treats four seats other than Ōanamuji as uncertain. ''Yabu Gunshi'' (1928) gives the upper shrine deities as {{jalink|Ukemochi|Q2280323}} and {{jalink|Isotakeru|Q10881556}}, the middle shrine as Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine as Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and Funahotari-no-Mikoto''Gods of Japan (7) San'in'', p.331. === History === Tradition says the shrine was founded in the 30th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, and it is already named together with {{jalink|Izushi Shrine|Q3156511}} and {{jalink|Awaga Shrine|Q11604651}} in the ''Tajima no Kuni Zeiseichō'' of {{jalink|Tenpyō|Q1199879}} 9 ({{jalink|737|Q23583}}). For divine court rank, it received junior fifth rank lower in {{jalink|Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 12 (845) (''{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301}}''), rose to senior fifth rank lower in Jōgan 11 (869), and to senior fifth rank upper in Jōgan 16 (874) (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}''). The ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' records "Yabufuza Shrine, five seats (two myōjin-tai and three small seats)". In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, Suisenzan Fugen-ji served as the attached temple. In {{jalink|1814|Q6943}} (Bunka 11), {{jalink|Ino Tadataka|Q1240937}} visited during his {{jalink|surveying|Q816425}} expedition. === Precinct === File:Yabu-jinja, shaden-2.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Yabu-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall File:Yabu-jinja, tou.jpg|Stone hōkyōintō pagoda (Yabu City designated cultural property) File:Yabu-jinja, Yamanokuchi-jinja.jpg|Yamanokuchi Shrine File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Festivals === * {{jalink|February 25|Q2356}} Spring Grand Festival ({{jalink|Kinen-sai|Q118906710}}) * Third Sunday of April and preceding day: {{jalink|Ohashiri-san|Q11263474|Oshiri Festival}} - designated intangible folk cultural property of Yabu City * {{jalink|May 2|Q2545}} {{jalink|Hachijūhachiya|Q11390609}} Festival * {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} Autumn Grand Festival ({{jalink|annual festival|Q11385469}}) === Cultural properties === === Registered cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture === * Tangible cultural properties ** Yabu Shrine, 5 structures (buildings) - registered March 5, 2010 (Heisei 22){{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/29kentouroku.pdf |title=List of prefecturally registered cultural properties |website=Hyogo Prefectural Education and Training Institute |accessdate=2020-01-13}}. *** Main hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Worship hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Yamanokuchi Shrine - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Gosha Shrine - built in the late Edo period. === Yabu City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Stone hōkyōintō pagoda - designated May 18, 1977 (Showa 52)Explanation board for the stone hōkyōintō pagoda.. * Intangible folk cultural properties ** Oshiri Festival === Access === * Rail / bus ** About 2 km on foot from {{jalink|Yabu Station|Q871175}} on the JR {{jalink|San'in Main Line|Q1135170}} ** From JR San'in Main Line Yabu Station, take {{jalink|Zentan Bus|Q11389110}} bound for "Yōka Station" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" ** From JR San'in Main Line {{jalink|Yōka Station|Q870988}}, take Zentan Bus bound for "Wadayama / Yamaguchi" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" * Roads ** About 1 hour from {{jalink|Fukuchiyama Interchange|Q11594317|Fukuchiyama IC}} on the {{jalink|Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway|Q11613730}} ** About 20 minutes from the Asago-side exit of the {{jalink|Tōen Tunnel Toll Road}} ** About 15 minutes from {{jalink|Wadayama Interchange|Q10921611|Wadayama IC}} on the {{jalink|Bantan Renraku Road|Q11498699}} ** About 5 minutes from {{jalink|Michi-no-Eki Yabu|Q11640978}} and {{jalink|Michi-no-Eki Tajima Rakuza|Q11641146}} === Nearby === * {{jalink|Ōyabu Kofun Group|Q83458374}} * Hotaru no Sato * {{jalink|Manpuku-ji (Yabu)|Q43594125|Manpuku-ji}} - a famous temple known as "Kōya of Tajima" === Notes === {{reflist}} === References == = * Edited by {{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San'in'' (new reprint), 2000, {{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}, ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 * ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 19 San'indō 2, Kogakkan University Press, 1984 * ''Hyōgo-ken Jinja-shi'', lower volume (reprint of 1938 edition published by Hyogo Shrine Priests' Association), Rinkawa Shoten, 1984, ISBN 4-653-01102-8 * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1175348 ''Tango Shiryo Sosho'' Vol. 4], Tango Shiryo Sosho Kankokai, 1927, National Diet Library Digital Collections (accessed June 17, 2025) ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Yabu-jinja}} * [http://www.yabu-jinja.jp/ Yabu Shrine] - content == {{神社 |名称 = Yabu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yabu-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{jalink|Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Yabu|Q869014}} Ichiba 840, Yabu City |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|14.6|N|134|48|13.7|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Tanba Michinushi no Kimi||Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}}
Funahotari-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|myōjin-taisha}}: 2 seats, small: 3 seats)
{{jalink|Tajima Province|Q1053727}} {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379|Sannomiya}}
former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = (tradition) 30th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} |本殿 = {{jalink|irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} with {{jalink|hinoki bark roofing|Q90373477}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |神事 = Oshiri Festival (third Sunday of April and the preceding day) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Yabu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yabu-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Precinct entrance}}]] '''Yabu Shrine''' (やぶじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Ichiba, {{jalink|Yabu|Q869014}}, {{jalink|Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290}}. It is the {{jalink|Tajima Province|Q1053727}} {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379|third-ranking provincial shrine (sannomiya)}}, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (2 myōjin-taisha seats and 3 small-shrine seats), and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|kensha}}. It is popularly called "Yabu no Myojin-san" and is known as a deity of agriculture. Ichiba in Yabu has long been a center of cattle markets for {{jalink|Tajima cattle|Q13417172}}, and auctions are still held at nearby Ōyabu. === Enshrined deities === The five currently enshrined seats are: * {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Tanba Michinushi no Kimi||Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}} * Funahotari-no-Mikoto - descendant of Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and ancestor of the provincial rulers of Tajima In ''Tajima-kō'', a regional record compiled in the Edo period, it is described that once the upper shrine on Mt. Yadaka enshrined Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto, the middle shrine on the mid-slope enshrined Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto and Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine at the present site enshrined Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto. ''{{jalink|Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092}}'' treats four seats other than Ōanamuji as uncertain. ''Yabu Gunshi'' (1928) gives the upper shrine deities as {{jalink|Ukemochi|Q2280323}} and {{jalink|Isotakeru|Q10881556}}, the middle shrine as Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine as Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and Funahotari-no-Mikoto''Gods of Japan (7) San'in'', p.331. === History === Tradition says the shrine was founded in the 30th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, and it is already named together with {{jalink|Izushi Shrine|Q3156511}} and {{jalink|Awaga Shrine|Q11604651}} in the ''Tajima no Kuni Zeiseichō'' of {{jalink|Tenpyō|Q1199879}} 9 ({{jalink|737|Q23583}}). For divine court rank, it received junior fifth rank lower in {{jalink|Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 12 (845) (''{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301}}''), rose to senior fifth rank lower in Jōgan 11 (869), and to senior fifth rank upper in Jōgan 16 (874) (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}''). The ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' records "Yabufuza Shrine, five seats (two myōjin-tai and three small seats)". In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, Suisenzan Fugen-ji served as the attached temple. In {{jalink|1814|Q6943}} (Bunka 11), {{jalink|Ino Tadataka|Q1240937}} visited during his {{jalink|surveying|Q816425}} expedition. === Precinct === File:Yabu-jinja, shaden-2.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Yabu-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall File:Yabu-jinja, tou.jpg|Stone hōkyōintō pagoda (Yabu City designated cultural property) File:Yabu-jinja, Yamanokuchi-jinja.jpg|Yamanokuchi Shrine File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Festivals === * {{jalink|February 25|Q2356}} Spring Grand Festival ({{jalink|Kinen-sai|Q118906710}}) * Third Sunday of April and preceding day: {{jalink|Ohashiri-san|Q11263474|Oshiri Festival}} - designated intangible folk cultural property of Yabu City * {{jalink|May 2|Q2545}} {{jalink|Hachijūhachiya|Q11390609}} Festival * {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} Autumn Grand Festival ({{jalink|annual festival|Q11385469}}) === Cultural properties === === Registered cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture === * Tangible cultural properties ** Yabu Shrine, 5 structures (buildings) - registered March 5, 2010 (Heisei 22){{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/29kentouroku.pdf |title=List of prefecturally registered cultural properties |website=Hyogo Prefectural Education and Training Institute |accessdate=2020-01-13}}. *** Main hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Worship hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Yamanokuchi Shrine - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Gosha Shrine - built in the late Edo period. === Yabu City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Stone hōkyōintō pagoda - designated May 18, 1977 (Showa 52)Explanation board for the stone hōkyōintō pagoda.. * Intangible folk cultural properties ** Oshiri Festival === Access === * Rail / bus ** About 2 km on foot from {{jalink|Yabu Station|Q871175}} on the JR {{jalink|San'in Main Line|Q1135170}} ** From JR San'in Main Line Yabu Station, take {{jalink|Zentan Bus|Q11389110}} bound for "Yōka Station" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" ** From JR San'in Main Line {{jalink|Yōka Station|Q870988}}, take Zentan Bus bound for "Wadayama / Yamaguchi" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" * Roads ** About 1 hour from {{jalink|Fukuchiyama Interchange|Q11594317|Fukuchiyama IC}} on the {{jalink|Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway|Q11613730}} ** About 20 minutes from the Asago-side exit of the {{jalink|Tōen Tunnel Toll Road}} ** About 15 minutes from {{jalink|Wadayama Interchange|Q10921611|Wadayama IC}} on the {{jalink|Bantan Renraku Road|Q11498699}} ** About 5 minutes from {{jalink|Michi-no-Eki Yabu|Q11640978}} and {{jalink|Michi-no-Eki Tajima Rakuza|Q11641146}} === Nearby === * {{jalink|Ōyabu Kofun Group|Q83458374}} * Hotaru no Sato * {{jalink|Manpuku-ji (Yabu)|Q43594125|Manpuku-ji}} - a famous temple known as "Kōya of Tajima" === Notes === {{reflist}} === References == = * Edited by {{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San'in'' (new reprint), 2000, {{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}, ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 * ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 19 San'indō 2, Kogakkan University Press, 1984 * ''Hyōgo-ken Jinja-shi'', lower volume (reprint of 1938 edition published by Hyogo Shrine Priests' Association), Rinkawa Shoten, 1984, ISBN 4-653-01102-8 * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1175348 ''Tango Shiryo Sosho'' Vol. 4], Tango Shiryo Sosho Kankokai, 1927, National Diet Library Digital Collections (accessed June 17, 2025) ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Yabu-jinja}} * [http://www.yabu-jinja.jp/ Yabu Shrine] - Funahotari-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|myōjin-taisha}}: 2 seats, small: 3 seats)
{{jalink|Tajima Province|Q1053727}} {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379|Sannomiya}}
former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建 = (tradition) 30th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} |本殿 = {{jalink|irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} with {{jalink|hinoki bark roofing|Q90373477}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |神事 = Oshiri Festival (third Sunday of April and the preceding day) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Yabu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yabu-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Precinct entrance}}]] '''Yabu Shrine''' (やぶじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Ichiba, {{jalink|Yabu|Q869014}}, {{jalink|Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290}}. It is the {{jalink|Tajima Province|Q1053727}} {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379|third-ranking provincial shrine (sannomiya)}}, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (2 myōjin-taisha seats and 3 small-shrine seats), and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|kensha}}. It is popularly called "Yabu no Myojin-san" and is known as a deity of agriculture. Ichiba in Yabu has long been a center of cattle markets for {{jalink|Tajima cattle|Q13417172}}, and auctions are still held at nearby Ōyabu. === Enshrined deities === The five currently enshrined seats are: * {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Tanba Michinushi no Kimi||Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}} * Funahotari-no-Mikoto - descendant of Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and ancestor of the provincial rulers of Tajima In ''Tajima-kō'', a regional record compiled in the Edo period, it is described that once the upper shrine on Mt. Yadaka enshrined Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto, the middle shrine on the mid-slope enshrined Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto and Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine at the present site enshrined Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto. ''{{jalink|Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092}}'' treats four seats other than Ōanamuji as uncertain. ''Yabu Gunshi'' (1928) gives the upper shrine deities as {{jalink|Ukemochi|Q2280323}} and {{jalink|Isotakeru|Q10881556}}, the middle shrine as Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine as Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and Funahotari-no-Mikoto''Gods of Japan (7) San'in'', p.331. === History === Tradition says the shrine was founded in the 30th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, and it is already named together with {{jalink|Izushi Shrine|Q3156511}} and {{jalink|Awaga Shrine|Q11604651}} in the ''Tajima no Kuni Zeiseichō'' of {{jalink|Tenpyō|Q1199879}} 9 ({{jalink|737|Q23583}}). For divine court rank, it received junior fifth rank lower in {{jalink|Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 12 (845) (''{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301}}''), rose to senior fifth rank lower in Jōgan 11 (869), and to senior fifth rank upper in Jōgan 16 (874) (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}''). The ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' records "Yabufuza Shrine, five seats (two myōjin-tai and three small seats)". In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, Suisenzan Fugen-ji served as the attached temple. In {{jalink|1814|Q6943}} (Bunka 11), {{jalink|Ino Tadataka|Q1240937}} visited during his {{jalink|surveying|Q816425}} expedition. === Precinct === File:Yabu-jinja, shaden-2.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Yabu-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall File:Yabu-jinja, tou.jpg|Stone hōkyōintō pagoda (Yabu City designated cultural property) File:Yabu-jinja, Yamanokuchi-jinja.jpg|Yamanokuchi Shrine File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Festivals === * {{jalink|February 25|Q2356}} Spring Grand Festival ({{jalink|Kinen-sai|Q118906710}}) * Third Sunday of April and preceding day: {{jalink|Ohashiri-san|Q11263474|Oshiri Festival}} - designated intangible folk cultural property of Yabu City * {{jalink|May 2|Q2545}} {{jalink|Hachijūhachiya|Q11390609}} Festival * {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} Autumn Grand Festival ({{jalink|annual festival|Q11385469}}) === Cultural properties === === Registered cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture === * Tangible cultural properties ** Yabu Shrine, 5 structures (buildings) - registered March 5, 2010 (Heisei 22){{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/29kentouroku.pdf |title=List of prefecturally registered cultural properties |website=Hyogo Prefectural Education and Training Institute |accessdate=2020-01-13}}. *** Main hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Worship hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Yamanokuchi Shrine - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Gosha Shrine - built in the late Edo period. === Yabu City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Stone hōkyōintō pagoda - designated May 18, 1977 (Showa 52)Explanation board for the stone hōkyōintō pagoda.. * Intangible folk cultural properties ** Oshiri Festival === Access === * Rail / bus ** About 2 km on foot from {{jalink|Yabu Station|Q871175}} on the JR {{jalink|San'in Main Line|Q1135170}} ** From JR San'in Main Line Yabu Station, take {{jalink|Zentan Bus|Q11389110}} bound for "Yōka Station" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" ** From JR San'in Main Line {{jalink|Yōka Station|Q870988}}, take Zentan Bus bound for "Wadayama / Yamaguchi" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" * Roads ** About 1 hour from {{jalink|Fukuchiyama Interchange|Q11594317|Fukuchiyama IC}} on the {{jalink|Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway|Q11613730}} ** About 20 minutes from the Asago-side exit of the {{jalink|Tōen Tunnel Toll Road}} ** About 15 minutes from {{jalink|Wadayama Interchange|Q10921611|Wadayama IC}} on the {{jalink|Bantan Renraku Road|Q11498699}} ** About 5 minutes from {{jalink|Michi-no-Eki Yabu|Q11640978}} and {{jalink|Michi-no-Eki Tajima Rakuza|Q11641146}} === Nearby === * {{jalink|Ōyabu Kofun Group|Q83458374}} * Hotaru no Sato * {{jalink|Manpuku-ji (Yabu)|Q43594125|Manpuku-ji}} - a famous temple known as "Kōya of Tajima" === Notes === {{reflist}} === References == = * Edited by {{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San'in'' (new reprint), 2000, {{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}, ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 * ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 19 San'indō 2, Kogakkan University Press, 1984 * ''Hyōgo-ken Jinja-shi'', lower volume (reprint of 1938 edition published by Hyogo Shrine Priests' Association), Rinkawa Shoten, 1984, ISBN 4-653-01102-8 * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1175348 ''Tango Shiryo Sosho'' Vol. 4], Tango Shiryo Sosho Kankokai, 1927, National Diet Library Digital Collections (accessed June 17, 2025) ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Yabu-jinja}} * [http://www.yabu-jinja.jp/ Yabu Shrine] - official site * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/380201.html Yabufuza Shrine, Five Seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:養父市の建築物]] [[Category:養父市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:但馬国の式内社|名やふしんしや]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:但馬国|社3]][[Category:qq]]

designated May 18, 1977 (Showa 52)Explanation board for the stone hōkyōintō pagoda.. * Intangible folk cultural properties ** Oshiri Festival === Access === * Rail / bus ** About 2 km on foot from {{jalink|Yabu Station|Q871175}} on the JR {{jalink|San'in Main Line|Q1135170}} ** From JR San'in Main Line Yabu Station, take {{jalink|Zentan Bus|Q11389110}} bound for "Yōka Station" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" ** From JR San'in Main Line {{jalink|Yōka Station|Q870988}}, take Zentan Bus bound for "Wadayama / Yamaguchi" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" * Roads ** About 1 hour from {{jalink|Fukuchiyama Interchange|Q11594317|Fukuchiyama IC}} on the {{jalink|Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway|Q11613730}} ** About 20 minutes from the Asago-side exit of the {{jalink|Tōen Tunnel Toll Road}} ** About 15 minutes from {{jalink|Wadayama Interchange|Q10921611|Wadayama IC}} on the {{jalink|Bantan Renraku Road|Q11498699}} ** About 5 minutes from {{jalink|Michi-no-Eki Yabu|Q11640978}} and {{jalink|Michi-no-Eki Tajima Rakuza|Q11641146}} === Nearby === * {{jalink|Ōyabu Kofun Group|Q83458374}} * Hotaru no Sato * {{jalink|Manpuku-ji (Yabu)|Q43594125|Manpuku-ji}} - a famous temple known as "Kōya of Tajima" === Notes === {{reflist}} === References == = * Edited by {{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San'in'' (new reprint), 2000, {{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}, ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 * ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 19 San'indō 2, Kogakkan University Press, 1984 * ''Hyōgo-ken Jinja-shi'', lower volume (reprint of 1938 edition published by Hyogo Shrine Priests' Association), Rinkawa Shoten, 1984, ISBN 4-653-01102-8 * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1175348 ''Tango Shiryo Sosho'' Vol. 4], Tango Shiryo Sosho Kankokai, 1927, National Diet Library Digital Collections (accessed June 17, 2025) ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Yabu-jinja}} * [http://www.yabu-jinja.jp/ Yabu Shrine] - content == {{神社 |名称 = Yabu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yabu-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Yabu|Q869014|WD=Q869014}} Ichiba 840, Yabu City |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|14.6|N|134|48|13.7|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Kimi||WD=|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}}
Funahotari-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-taisha}}: 2 seats, small: 3 seats)
{{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya}}
former {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (tradition) 30th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} with {{ill|lt=hinoki bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |神事 = Oshiri Festival (third Sunday of April and the preceding day) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Yabu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yabu-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Precinct entrance}}]] '''Yabu Shrine''' (やぶじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Ichiba, {{ill|lt=Yabu|Q869014|WD=Q869014}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}. It is the {{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=third-ranking provincial shrine (sannomiya)}}, a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (2 myōjin-taisha seats and 3 small-shrine seats), and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} system it was a {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. It is popularly called "Yabu no Myojin-san" and is known as a deity of agriculture. Ichiba in Yabu has long been a center of cattle markets for {{ill|lt=Tajima cattle|Q13417172|WD=Q13417172}}, and auctions are still held at nearby Ōyabu. === Enshrined deities === The five currently enshrined seats are: * {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Kimi||WD=|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}} * Funahotari-no-Mikoto - descendant of Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and ancestor of the provincial rulers of Tajima In ''Tajima-kō'', a regional record compiled in the Edo period, it is described that once the upper shrine on Mt. Yadaka enshrined Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto, the middle shrine on the mid-slope enshrined Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto and Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine at the present site enshrined Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto. ''{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092}}'' treats four seats other than Ōanamuji as uncertain. ''Yabu Gunshi'' (1928) gives the upper shrine deities as {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}} and {{ill|lt=Isotakeru|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556}}, the middle shrine as Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine as Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and Funahotari-no-Mikoto''Gods of Japan (7) San'in'', p.331. === History === Tradition says the shrine was founded in the 30th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, and it is already named together with {{ill|lt=Izushi Shrine|Q3156511|WD=Q3156511}} and {{ill|lt=Awaga Shrine|Q11604651|WD=Q11604651}} in the ''Tajima no Kuni Zeiseichō'' of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 9 ({{ill|lt=737|Q23583|WD=Q23583}}). For divine court rank, it received junior fifth rank lower in {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 12 (845) (''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}''), rose to senior fifth rank lower in Jōgan 11 (869), and to senior fifth rank upper in Jōgan 16 (874) (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}''). The ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' records "Yabufuza Shrine, five seats (two myōjin-tai and three small seats)". In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, Suisenzan Fugen-ji served as the attached temple. In {{ill|lt=1814|Q6943|WD=Q6943}} (Bunka 11), {{ill|lt=Ino Tadataka|Q1240937|WD=Q1240937}} visited during his {{ill|lt=surveying|Q816425|WD=Q816425}} expedition. === Precinct === File:Yabu-jinja, shaden-2.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Yabu-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall File:Yabu-jinja, tou.jpg|Stone hōkyōintō pagoda (Yabu City designated cultural property) File:Yabu-jinja, Yamanokuchi-jinja.jpg|Yamanokuchi Shrine File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Festivals === * {{ill|lt=February 25|Q2356|WD=Q2356}} Spring Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}}) * Third Sunday of April and preceding day: {{ill|lt=Ohashiri-san|Q11263474|WD=Q11263474|lt=Oshiri Festival}} - designated intangible folk cultural property of Yabu City * {{ill|lt=May 2|Q2545|WD=Q2545}} {{ill|lt=Hachijūhachiya|Q11390609|WD=Q11390609}} Festival * {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}}) === Cultural properties === === Registered cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture === * Tangible cultural properties ** Yabu Shrine, 5 structures (buildings) - registered March 5, 2010 (Heisei 22){{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/29kentouroku.pdf |title=List of prefecturally registered cultural properties |website=Hyogo Prefectural Education and Training Institute |accessdate=2020-01-13}}. *** Main hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Worship hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Yamanokuchi Shrine - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Gosha Shrine - built in the late Edo period. === Yabu City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Stone hōkyōintō pagoda - designated May 18, 1977 (Showa 52)Explanation board for the stone hōkyōintō pagoda.. * Intangible folk cultural properties ** Oshiri Festival === Access === * Rail / bus ** About 2 km on foot from {{ill|lt=Yabu Station|Q871175|WD=Q871175}} on the JR {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170}} ** From JR San'in Main Line Yabu Station, take {{ill|lt=Zentan Bus|Q11389110|WD=Q11389110}} bound for "Yōka Station" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" ** From JR San'in Main Line {{ill|lt=Yōka Station|Q870988|WD=Q870988}}, take Zentan Bus bound for "Wadayama / Yamaguchi" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" * Roads ** About 1 hour from {{ill|lt=Fukuchiyama Interchange|Q11594317|WD=Q11594317|lt=Fukuchiyama IC}} on the {{ill|lt=Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway|Q11613730|WD=Q11613730}} ** About 20 minutes from the Asago-side exit of the {{ill||lt=Tōen Tunnel Toll Road|UNKNOWN}} ** About 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Wadayama Interchange|Q10921611|WD=Q10921611|lt=Wadayama IC}} on the {{ill|lt=Bantan Renraku Road|Q11498699|WD=Q11498699}} ** About 5 minutes from {{ill|lt=Michi-no-Eki Yabu|Q11640978|WD=Q11640978}} and {{ill|lt=Michi-no-Eki Tajima Rakuza|Q11641146|WD=Q11641146}} === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Ōyabu Kofun Group|Q83458374|WD=Q83458374}} * Hotaru no Sato * {{ill|lt=Manpuku-ji (Yabu)|Q43594125|WD=Q43594125|lt=Manpuku-ji}} - a famous temple known as "Kōya of Tajima" === Notes === {{reflist}} === References == = * Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San'in'' (new reprint), 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}, ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 * ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 19 San'indō 2, Kogakkan University Press, 1984 * ''Hyōgo-ken Jinja-shi'', lower volume (reprint of 1938 edition published by Hyogo Shrine Priests' Association), Rinkawa Shoten, 1984, ISBN 4-653-01102-8 * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1175348 ''Tango Shiryo Sosho'' Vol. 4], Tango Shiryo Sosho Kankokai, 1927, National Diet Library Digital Collections (accessed June 17, 2025) ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Yabu-jinja}} * [http://www.yabu-jinja.jp/ Yabu Shrine] - {'amount': '+5', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県養父市養父市場840', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yabu-jinja}} [[JA:養父神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11666856}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yabu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yabu-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Yabu|Q869014|WD=Q869014}} Ichiba 840, Yabu City |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|14.6|N|134|48|13.7|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Kimi||WD=|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}}
Funahotari-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-taisha}}: 2 seats, small: 3 seats)
{{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (tradition) 30th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} with {{ill|lt=hinoki bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |神事 = Oshiri Festival (third Sunday of April and the preceding day) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Yabu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yabu-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Precinct entrance}}]] '''Yabu Shrine''' (やぶじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Ichiba, {{ill|lt=Yabu|Q869014|WD=Q869014}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}. It is the {{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=third-ranking provincial shrine (sannomiya)}}, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (2 myōjin-taisha seats and 3 small-shrine seats), and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} system it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. It is popularly called "Yabu no Myojin-san" and is known as a deity of agriculture. Ichiba in Yabu has long been a center of cattle markets for {{ill|lt=Tajima cattle|Q13417172|WD=Q13417172}}, and auctions are still held at nearby Ōyabu. === Enshrined deities === The five currently enshrined seats are: * {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Kimi||WD=|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}} * Funahotari-no-Mikoto - descendant of Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and ancestor of the provincial rulers of Tajima In ''Tajima-kō'', a regional record compiled in the Edo period, it is described that once the upper shrine on Mt. Yadaka enshrined Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto, the middle shrine on the mid-slope enshrined Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto and Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine at the present site enshrined Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto. ''{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092}}'' treats four seats other than Ōanamuji as uncertain. ''Yabu Gunshi'' (1928) gives the upper shrine deities as {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}} and {{ill|lt=Isotakeru|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556}}, the middle shrine as Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine as Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and Funahotari-no-Mikoto''Gods of Japan (7) San'in'', p.331. === History === Tradition says the shrine was founded in the 30th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, and it is already named together with {{ill|lt=Izushi Shrine|Q3156511|WD=Q3156511}} and {{ill|lt=Awaga Shrine|Q11604651|WD=Q11604651}} in the ''Tajima no Kuni Zeiseichō'' of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 9 ({{ill|lt=737|Q23583|WD=Q23583}}). For divine court rank, it received junior fifth rank lower in {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 12 (845) (''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}''), rose to senior fifth rank lower in Jōgan 11 (869), and to senior fifth rank upper in Jōgan 16 (874) (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}''). The ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' records "Yabufuza Shrine, five seats (two myōjin-tai and three small seats)". In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, Suisenzan Fugen-ji served as the attached temple. In {{ill|lt=1814|Q6943|WD=Q6943}} (Bunka 11), {{ill|lt=Ino Tadataka|Q1240937|WD=Q1240937}} visited during his {{ill|lt=surveying|Q816425|WD=Q816425}} expedition. === Precinct === File:Yabu-jinja, shaden-2.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Yabu-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall File:Yabu-jinja, tou.jpg|Stone hōkyōintō pagoda (Yabu City designated cultural property) File:Yabu-jinja, Yamanokuchi-jinja.jpg|Yamanokuchi Shrine File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Festivals === * {{ill|lt=February 25|Q2356|WD=Q2356}} Spring Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}}) * Third Sunday of April and preceding day: {{ill|lt=Ohashiri-san|Q11263474|WD=Q11263474|lt=Oshiri Festival}} - designated intangible folk cultural property of Yabu City * {{ill|lt=May 2|Q2545|WD=Q2545}} {{ill|lt=Hachijūhachiya|Q11390609|WD=Q11390609}} Festival * {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}}) === Cultural properties === === Registered cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture === * Tangible cultural properties ** Yabu Shrine, 5 structures (buildings) - registered March 5, 2010 (Heisei 22){{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/29kentouroku.pdf |title=List of prefecturally registered cultural properties |website=Hyogo Prefectural Education and Training Institute |accessdate=2020-01-13}}. *** Main hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Worship hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Yamanokuchi Shrine - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Gosha Shrine - built in the late Edo period. === Yabu City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Stone hōkyōintō pagoda - designated May 18, 1977 (Showa 52)Explanation board for the stone hōkyōintō pagoda.. * Intangible folk cultural properties ** Oshiri Festival === Access === * Rail / bus ** About 2 km on foot from {{ill|lt=Yabu Station|Q871175|WD=Q871175}} on the JR {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170}} ** From JR San'in Main Line Yabu Station, take {{ill|lt=Zentan Bus|Q11389110|WD=Q11389110}} bound for "Yōka Station" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" ** From JR San'in Main Line {{ill|lt=Yōka Station|Q870988|WD=Q870988}}, take Zentan Bus bound for "Wadayama / Yamaguchi" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" * Roads ** About 1 hour from {{ill|lt=Fukuchiyama Interchange|Q11594317|WD=Q11594317|lt=Fukuchiyama IC}} on the {{ill|lt=Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway|Q11613730|WD=Q11613730}} ** About 20 minutes from the Asago-side exit of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tōen Tunnel Toll Road|UNKNOWN}} ** About 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Wadayama Interchange|Q10921611|WD=Q10921611|lt=Wadayama IC}} on the {{ill|lt=Bantan Renraku Road|Q11498699|WD=Q11498699}} ** About 5 minutes from {{ill|lt=Michi-no-Eki Yabu|Q11640978|WD=Q11640978}} and {{ill|lt=Michi-no-Eki Tajima Rakuza|Q11641146|WD=Q11641146}} === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Ōyabu Kofun Group|Q83458374|WD=Q83458374}} * Hotaru no Sato * {{ill|lt=Manpuku-ji (Yabu)|Q43594125|WD=Q43594125|lt=Manpuku-ji}} - a famous temple known as "Kōya of Tajima" === Notes === {{reflist}} === References == = * Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San'in'' (new reprint), 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}, ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 * ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 19 San'indō 2, Kogakkan University Press, 1984 * ''Hyōgo-ken Jinja-shi'', lower volume (reprint of 1938 edition published by Hyogo Shrine Priests' Association), Rinkawa Shoten, 1984, ISBN 4-653-01102-8 * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1175348 ''Tango Shiryo Sosho'' Vol. 4], Tango Shiryo Sosho Kankokai, 1927, National Diet Library Digital Collections (accessed June 17, 2025) ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Yabu-jinja}} * [http://www.yabu-jinja.jp/ Yabu Shrine] - official site * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/380201.html Yabufuza Shrine, Five Seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:養父市の建築物]] [[Category:養父市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:但馬国の式内社|名やふしんしや]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:但馬国|社3]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Yabu|Q869014|WD=Q869014}} Ichiba 840, Yabu City |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|14.6|N|134|48|13.7|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}

{{ill|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Kimi||WD=|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}}
Funahotari-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-taisha}}: 2 seats, small: 3 seats)
{{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (tradition) 30th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} with {{ill|lt=hinoki bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |神事 = Oshiri Festival (third Sunday of April and the preceding day) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Yabu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yabu-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Precinct entrance}}]] '''Yabu Shrine''' (やぶじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Ichiba, {{ill|lt=Yabu|Q869014|WD=Q869014}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}. It is the {{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=third-ranking provincial shrine (sannomiya)}}, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (2 myōjin-taisha seats and 3 small-shrine seats), and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} system it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is popularly called "Yabu no Myojin-san" and is known as a deity of agriculture. Ichiba in Yabu has long been a center of cattle markets for {{ill|lt=Tajima cattle|Q13417172|WD=Q13417172}}, and auctions are still held at nearby Ōyabu. === Enshrined deities === The five currently enshrined seats are: * {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Kimi||WD=|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}} * Funahotari-no-Mikoto - descendant of Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and ancestor of the provincial rulers of Tajima In ''Tajima-kō'', a regional record compiled in the Edo period, it is described that once the upper shrine on Mt. Yadaka enshrined Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto, the middle shrine on the mid-slope enshrined Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto and Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine at the present site enshrined Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto. ''{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092}}'' treats four seats other than Ōanamuji as uncertain. ''Yabu Gunshi'' (1928) gives the upper shrine deities as {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}} and {{ill|lt=Isotakeru|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556}}, the middle shrine as Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine as Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and Funahotari-no-Mikoto''Gods of Japan (7) San'in'', p.331. === History === Tradition says the shrine was founded in the 30th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, and it is already named together with {{ill|lt=Izushi Shrine|Q3156511|WD=Q3156511}} and {{ill|lt=Awaga Shrine|Q11604651|WD=Q11604651}} in the ''Tajima no Kuni Zeiseichō'' of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 9 ({{ill|lt=737|Q23583|WD=Q23583}}). For divine court rank, it received junior fifth rank lower in {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 12 (845) (''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}''), rose to senior fifth rank lower in Jōgan 11 (869), and to senior fifth rank upper in Jōgan 16 (874) (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}''). The ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' records "Yabufuza Shrine, five seats (two myōjin-tai and three small seats)". In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, Suisenzan Fugen-ji served as the attached temple. In {{ill|lt=1814|Q6943|WD=Q6943}} (Bunka 11), {{ill|lt=Ino Tadataka|Q1240937|WD=Q1240937}} visited during his {{ill|lt=surveying|Q816425|WD=Q816425}} expedition. === Precinct === File:Yabu-jinja, shaden-2.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Yabu-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall File:Yabu-jinja, tou.jpg|Stone hōkyōintō pagoda (Yabu City designated cultural property) File:Yabu-jinja, Yamanokuchi-jinja.jpg|Yamanokuchi Shrine File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Festivals === * {{ill|lt=February 25|Q2356|WD=Q2356}} Spring Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}}) * Third Sunday of April and preceding day: {{ill|lt=Ohashiri-san|Q11263474|WD=Q11263474|lt=Oshiri Festival}} - designated intangible folk cultural property of Yabu City * {{ill|lt=May 2|Q2545|WD=Q2545}} {{ill|lt=Hachijūhachiya|Q11390609|WD=Q11390609}} Festival * {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}}) === Cultural properties === === Registered cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture === * Tangible cultural properties ** Yabu Shrine, 5 structures (buildings) - registered March 5, 2010 (Heisei 22){{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/29kentouroku.pdf |title=List of prefecturally registered cultural properties |website=Hyogo Prefectural Education and Training Institute |accessdate=2020-01-13}}. *** Main hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Worship hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Yamanokuchi Shrine - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Gosha Shrine - built in the late Edo period. === Yabu City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Stone hōkyōintō pagoda - designated May 18, 1977 (Showa 52)Explanation board for the stone hōkyōintō pagoda.. * Intangible folk cultural properties ** Oshiri Festival === Access === * Rail / bus ** About 2 km on foot from {{ill|lt=Yabu Station|Q871175|WD=Q871175}} on the JR {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170}} ** From JR San'in Main Line Yabu Station, take {{ill|lt=Zentan Bus|Q11389110|WD=Q11389110}} bound for "Yōka Station" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" ** From JR San'in Main Line {{ill|lt=Yōka Station|Q870988|WD=Q870988}}, take Zentan Bus bound for "Wadayama / Yamaguchi" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" * Roads ** About 1 hour from {{ill|lt=Fukuchiyama Interchange|Q11594317|WD=Q11594317|lt=Fukuchiyama IC}} on the {{ill|lt=Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway|Q11613730|WD=Q11613730}} ** About 20 minutes from the Asago-side exit of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tōen Tunnel Toll Road|1=UNKNOWN}} ** About 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Wadayama Interchange|Q10921611|WD=Q10921611|lt=Wadayama IC}} on the {{ill|lt=Bantan Renraku Road|Q11498699|WD=Q11498699}} ** About 5 minutes from {{ill|lt=Michi-no-Eki Yabu|Q11640978|WD=Q11640978}} and {{ill|lt=Michi-no-Eki Tajima Rakuza|Q11641146|WD=Q11641146}} === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Ōyabu Kofun Group|Q83458374|WD=Q83458374}} * Hotaru no Sato * {{ill|lt=Manpuku-ji (Yabu)|Q43594125|WD=Q43594125|lt=Manpuku-ji}} - a famous temple known as "Kōya of Tajima" === Notes === {{reflist}} === References == = * Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San'in'' (new reprint), 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}, ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 * ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 19 San'indō 2, Kogakkan University Press, 1984 * ''Hyōgo-ken Jinja-shi'', lower volume (reprint of 1938 edition published by Hyogo Shrine Priests' Association), Rinkawa Shoten, 1984, ISBN 4-653-01102-8 * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1175348 ''Tango Shiryo Sosho'' Vol. 4], Tango Shiryo Sosho Kankokai, 1927, National Diet Library Digital Collections (accessed June 17, 2025) ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Yabu-jinja}} * [http://www.yabu-jinja.jp/ Yabu Shrine] - official site * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/380201.html Yabufuza Shrine, Five Seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:養父市の建築物]] [[Category:養父市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:但馬国の式内社|名やふしんしや]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:但馬国|社3]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
