seat|WD=Q135018062|1=Engishiki seat}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+5', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県養父市養父市場840', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yabu-jinja}} [[JA:養父神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11666856}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yabu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yabu-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yabu|Q869014|WD=Q869014|1=Yabu, Hyōgo}} Ichiba 840, Yabu City |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|14.6|N|134|48|13.7|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}
{{ill|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Kimi|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}}
Funahotari-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-taisha|1=Myōjin Taisha}}: 2 seats, small: 3 seats)
{{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727|1=Tajima Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (tradition) 30th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} with {{ill|lt=hinoki bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事 = Oshiri Festival (third Sunday of April and the preceding day) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Yabu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yabu-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Precinct entrance}}]] '''Yabu Shrine''' (やぶじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ichiba, {{ill|lt=Yabu|Q869014|WD=Q869014|1=Yabu, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is the {{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727|1=Tajima Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=third-ranking provincial shrine (sannomiya)|1=Ichinomiya}}, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (2 myōjin-taisha seats and 3 small-shrine seats), and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is popularly called "Yabu no Myojin-san" and is known as a deity of agriculture. Ichiba in Yabu has long been a center of cattle markets for {{ill|lt=Tajima cattle|Q13417172|WD=Q13417172|1=Tajima cattle}}, and auctions are still held at nearby Ōyabu. === Enshrined deities === The five currently enshrined seats are: * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} * {{ill|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Kimi|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}} * Funahotari-no-Mikoto - descendant of Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and ancestor of the provincial rulers of Tajima In ''Tajima-kō'', a regional record compiled in the Edo period, it is described that once the upper shrine on Mt. Yadaka enshrined Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto, the middle shrine on the mid-slope enshrined Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto and Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine at the present site enshrined Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto. ''{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}'' treats four seats other than Ōanamuji as uncertain. ''Yabu Gunshi'' (1928) gives the upper shrine deities as {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}} and {{ill|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, the middle shrine as Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine as Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and Funahotari-no-Mikoto''Gods of Japan (7) San'in'', p.331. === History === Tradition says the shrine was founded in the 30th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and it is already named together with {{ill|lt=Izushi Shrine|Q3156511|WD=Q3156511|1=Izushi Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Awaga Shrine|Q11604651|WD=Q11604651|1=Awaga Shrine}} in the ''Tajima no Kuni Zeiseichō'' of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 9 ({{ill|lt=737|Q23583|WD=Q23583|1=AD 737}}). For divine court rank, it received junior fifth rank lower in {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 12 (845) (''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}''), rose to senior fifth rank lower in Jōgan 11 (869), and to senior fifth rank upper in Jōgan 16 (874) (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}''). The ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' records "Yabufuza Shrine, five seats (two myōjin-tai and three small seats)". In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, Suisenzan Fugen-ji served as the attached temple. In {{ill|lt=1814|Q6943|WD=Q6943|1=1814}} (Bunka 11), {{ill|lt=Ino Tadataka|Q1240937|WD=Q1240937|1=Inō Tadataka}} visited during his {{ill|lt=surveying|Q816425|WD=Q816425|1=Surveying}} expedition. === Precinct === File:Yabu-jinja, shaden-2.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Yabu-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall File:Yabu-jinja, tou.jpg|Stone hōkyōintō pagoda (Yabu City designated cultural property) File:Yabu-jinja, Yamanokuchi-jinja.jpg|Yamanokuchi Shrine File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Festivals === * {{ill|lt=February 25|Q2356|WD=Q2356|1=February 25}} Spring Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}) * Third Sunday of April and preceding day: {{ill|lt=Ohashiri-san|Q11263474|WD=Q11263474|lt=Oshiri Festival|1=Oshiri Festival}} - designated intangible folk cultural property of Yabu City * {{ill|lt=May 2|Q2545|WD=Q2545|1=May 2}} {{ill|lt=Hachijūhachiya|Q11390609|WD=Q11390609|1=Hachijūhachiya}} Festival * {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}}) === Cultural properties === === Registered cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture === * Tangible cultural properties ** Yabu Shrine, 5 structures (buildings) - registered March 5, 2010 (Heisei 22){{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/29kentouroku.pdf |title=List of prefecturally registered cultural properties |website=Hyogo Prefectural Education and Training Institute |accessdate=2020-01-13}}. *** Main hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Worship hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Yamanokuchi Shrine - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Gosha Shrine - built in the late Edo period. === Yabu City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Stone hōkyōintō pagoda - designated May 18, 1977 (Showa 52)Explanation board for the stone hōkyōintō pagoda.. * Intangible folk cultural properties ** Oshiri Festival === Access === * Rail / bus ** About 2 km on foot from {{ill|lt=Yabu Station|Q871175|WD=Q871175|1=Yabu Station}} on the JR {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}} ** From JR San'in Main Line Yabu Station, take {{ill|lt=Zentan Bus|Q11389110|WD=Q11389110|1=Zentan Bus}} bound for "Yōka Station" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" ** From JR San'in Main Line {{ill|lt=Yōka Station|Q870988|WD=Q870988|1=Yōka Station}}, take Zentan Bus bound for "Wadayama / Yamaguchi" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" * Roads ** About 1 hour from {{ill|lt=Fukuchiyama Interchange|Q11594317|WD=Q11594317|lt=Fukuchiyama IC|1=Fukuchiyama interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway|Q11613730|WD=Q11613730|1=Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway}} ** About 20 minutes from the Asago-side exit of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tōen Tunnel Toll Road|1=UNKNOWN}} ** About 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Wadayama Interchange|Q10921611|WD=Q10921611|lt=Wadayama IC|1=Wadayama interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Bantan Renraku Road|Q11498699|WD=Q11498699|1=Bantan Renraku Road}} ** About 5 minutes from {{ill|lt=Michi-no-Eki Yabu|Q11640978|WD=Q11640978|1=Roadside station Yabu}} and {{ill|lt=Michi-no-Eki Tajima Rakuza|Q11641146|WD=Q11641146|1=Roadside station Tajima-rakuza}} === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Ōyabu Kofun Group|Q83458374|WD=Q83458374|1=Oyabu Kofun Group}} * Hotaru no Sato * {{ill|lt=Manpuku-ji (Yabu)|Q43594125|WD=Q43594125|lt=Manpuku-ji|1=Manpuku-ji}} - a famous temple known as "Kōya of Tajima" === Notes === {{reflist}} === References == = * Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San'in'' (new reprint), 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 * ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 19 San'indō 2, Kogakkan University Press, 1984 * ''Hyōgo-ken Jinja-shi'', lower volume (reprint of 1938 edition published by Hyogo Shrine Priests' Association), Rinkawa Shoten, 1984, ISBN 4-653-01102-8 * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1175348 ''Tango Shiryo Sosho'' Vol. 4], Tango Shiryo Sosho Kankokai, 1927, National Diet Library Digital Collections (accessed June 17, 2025) ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Yabu-jinja}} * [http://www.yabu-jinja.jp/ Yabu Shrine] - official site * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/380201.html Yabufuza Shrine, Five Seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:養父市の建築物]] [[Category:養父市の歴史]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Tajima Province]]

[[JA:養父神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11666856}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yabu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yabu-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yabu|Q869014|WD=Q869014|1=Yabu, Hyōgo}} Ichiba 840, Yabu City |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|14.6|N|134|48|13.7|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}
{{ill|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Kimi|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}}
Funahotari-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-taisha|1=Myōjin Taisha}}: 2 seats, small: 3 seats)
{{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727|1=Tajima Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (tradition) 30th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} with {{ill|lt=hinoki bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事 = Oshiri Festival (third Sunday of April and the preceding day) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Yabu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yabu-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Precinct entrance}}]] '''Yabu Shrine''' (やぶじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ichiba, {{ill|lt=Yabu|Q869014|WD=Q869014|1=Yabu, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is the {{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727|1=Tajima Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=third-ranking provincial shrine (sannomiya)|1=Ichinomiya}}, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (2 myōjin-taisha seats and 3 small-shrine seats), and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is popularly called "Yabu no Myojin-san" and is known as a deity of agriculture. Ichiba in Yabu has long been a center of cattle markets for {{ill|lt=Tajima cattle|Q13417172|WD=Q13417172|1=Tajima cattle}}, and auctions are still held at nearby Ōyabu. === Enshrined deities === The five currently enshrined seats are: * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} * {{ill|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Kimi|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}} * Funahotari-no-Mikoto - descendant of Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and ancestor of the provincial rulers of Tajima In ''Tajima-kō'', a regional record compiled in the Edo period, it is described that once the upper shrine on Mt. Yadaka enshrined Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto, the middle shrine on the mid-slope enshrined Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto and Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine at the present site enshrined Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto. ''{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}'' treats four seats other than Ōanamuji as uncertain. ''Yabu Gunshi'' (1928) gives the upper shrine deities as {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}} and {{ill|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, the middle shrine as Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine as Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and Funahotari-no-Mikoto''Gods of Japan (7) San'in'', p.331. === History === Tradition says the shrine was founded in the 30th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and it is already named together with {{ill|lt=Izushi Shrine|Q3156511|WD=Q3156511|1=Izushi Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Awaga Shrine|Q11604651|WD=Q11604651|1=Awaga Shrine}} in the ''Tajima no Kuni Zeiseichō'' of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 9 ({{ill|lt=737|Q23583|WD=Q23583|1=AD 737}}). For divine court rank, it received junior fifth rank lower in {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 12 (845) (''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}''), rose to senior fifth rank lower in Jōgan 11 (869), and to senior fifth rank upper in Jōgan 16 (874) (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}''). The ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' records "Yabufuza Shrine, five seats (two myōjin-tai and three small seats)". In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, Suisenzan Fugen-ji served as the attached temple. In {{ill|lt=1814|Q6943|WD=Q6943|1=1814}} (Bunka 11), {{ill|lt=Ino Tadataka|Q1240937|WD=Q1240937|1=Inō Tadataka}} visited during his {{ill|lt=surveying|Q816425|WD=Q816425|1=Surveying}} expedition. === Precinct === File:Yabu-jinja, shaden-2.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Yabu-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall File:Yabu-jinja, tou.jpg|Stone hōkyōintō pagoda (Yabu City designated cultural property) File:Yabu-jinja, Yamanokuchi-jinja.jpg|Yamanokuchi Shrine File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Festivals === * {{ill|lt=February 25|Q2356|WD=Q2356|1=February 25}} Spring Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}) * Third Sunday of April and preceding day: {{ill|lt=Ohashiri-san|Q11263474|WD=Q11263474|lt=Oshiri Festival|1=Oshiri Festival}} - designated intangible folk cultural property of Yabu City * {{ill|lt=May 2|Q2545|WD=Q2545|1=May 2}} {{ill|lt=Hachijūhachiya|Q11390609|WD=Q11390609|1=Hachijūhachiya}} Festival * {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}}) === Cultural properties === === Registered cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture === * Tangible cultural properties ** Yabu Shrine, 5 structures (buildings) - registered March 5, 2010 (Heisei 22){{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/29kentouroku.pdf |title=List of prefecturally registered cultural properties |website=Hyogo Prefectural Education and Training Institute |accessdate=2020-01-13}}. *** Main hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Worship hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Yamanokuchi Shrine - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Gosha Shrine - built in the late Edo period. === Yabu City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Stone hōkyōintō pagoda - designated May 18, 1977 (Showa 52)Explanation board for the stone hōkyōintō pagoda.. * Intangible folk cultural properties ** Oshiri Festival === Access === * Rail / bus ** About 2 km on foot from {{ill|lt=Yabu Station|Q871175|WD=Q871175|1=Yabu Station}} on the JR {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}} ** From JR San'in Main Line Yabu Station, take {{ill|lt=Zentan Bus|Q11389110|WD=Q11389110|1=Zentan Bus}} bound for "Yōka Station" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" ** From JR San'in Main Line {{ill|lt=Yōka Station|Q870988|WD=Q870988|1=Yōka Station}}, take Zentan Bus bound for "Wadayama / Yamaguchi" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" * Roads ** About 1 hour from {{ill|lt=Fukuchiyama Interchange|Q11594317|WD=Q11594317|lt=Fukuchiyama IC|1=Fukuchiyama interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway|Q11613730|WD=Q11613730|1=Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway}} ** About 20 minutes from the Asago-side exit of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tōen Tunnel Toll Road|1=UNKNOWN}} ** About 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Wadayama Interchange|Q10921611|WD=Q10921611|lt=Wadayama IC|1=Wadayama interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Bantan Renraku Road|Q11498699|WD=Q11498699|1=Bantan Renraku Road}} ** About 5 minutes from {{ill|lt=Michi-no-Eki Yabu|Q11640978|WD=Q11640978|1=Roadside station Yabu}} and {{ill|lt=Michi-no-Eki Tajima Rakuza|Q11641146|WD=Q11641146|1=Roadside station Tajima-rakuza}} === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Ōyabu Kofun Group|Q83458374|WD=Q83458374|1=Oyabu Kofun Group}} * Hotaru no Sato * {{ill|lt=Manpuku-ji (Yabu)|Q43594125|WD=Q43594125|lt=Manpuku-ji|1=Manpuku-ji}} - a famous temple known as "Kōya of Tajima" === Notes === {{reflist}} === References == = * Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San'in'' (new reprint), 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 * ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 19 San'indō 2, Kogakkan University Press, 1984 * ''Hyōgo-ken Jinja-shi'', lower volume (reprint of 1938 edition published by Hyogo Shrine Priests' Association), Rinkawa Shoten, 1984, ISBN 4-653-01102-8 * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1175348 ''Tango Shiryo Sosho'' Vol. 4], Tango Shiryo Sosho Kankokai, 1927, National Diet Library Digital Collections (accessed June 17, 2025) ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Yabu-jinja}} * [http://www.yabu-jinja.jp/ Yabu Shrine] - official site * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/380201.html Yabufuza Shrine, Five Seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Yabu, Hyogo]] [[Category:養父市の歴史]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Tajima Province]]

[[JA:養父神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11666856}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yabu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yabu-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yabu|Q869014|WD=Q869014|1=Yabu, Hyōgo}} Ichiba 840, Yabu City |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|14.6|N|134|48|13.7|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}
{{ill|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Kimi|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}}
Funahotari-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-taisha|1=Myōjin Taisha}}: 2 seats, small: 3 seats)
{{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727|1=Tajima Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (tradition) 30th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} with {{ill|lt=hinoki bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事 = Oshiri Festival (third Sunday of April and the preceding day) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Yabu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yabu-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Precinct entrance}}]] '''Yabu Shrine''' (やぶじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ichiba, {{ill|lt=Yabu|Q869014|WD=Q869014|1=Yabu, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is the {{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727|1=Tajima Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=third-ranking provincial shrine (sannomiya)|1=Ichinomiya}}, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (2 myōjin-taisha seats and 3 small-shrine seats), and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is popularly called "Yabu no Myojin-san" and is known as a deity of agriculture. Ichiba in Yabu has long been a center of cattle markets for {{ill|lt=Tajima cattle|Q13417172|WD=Q13417172|1=Tajima cattle}}, and auctions are still held at nearby Ōyabu. === Enshrined deities === The five currently enshrined seats are: * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} * {{ill|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Kimi|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}} * Funahotari-no-Mikoto - descendant of Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and ancestor of the provincial rulers of Tajima In ''Tajima-kō'', a regional record compiled in the Edo period, it is described that once the upper shrine on Mt. Yadaka enshrined Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto, the middle shrine on the mid-slope enshrined Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto and Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine at the present site enshrined Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto. ''{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}'' treats four seats other than Ōanamuji as uncertain. ''Yabu Gunshi'' (1928) gives the upper shrine deities as {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}} and {{ill|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, the middle shrine as Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine as Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and Funahotari-no-Mikoto''Gods of Japan (7) San'in'', p.331. === History === Tradition says the shrine was founded in the 30th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and it is already named together with {{ill|lt=Izushi Shrine|Q3156511|WD=Q3156511|1=Izushi Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Awaga Shrine|Q11604651|WD=Q11604651|1=Awaga Shrine}} in the ''Tajima no Kuni Zeiseichō'' of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 9 ({{ill|lt=737|Q23583|WD=Q23583|1=AD 737}}). For divine court rank, it received junior fifth rank lower in {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 12 (845) (''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}''), rose to senior fifth rank lower in Jōgan 11 (869), and to senior fifth rank upper in Jōgan 16 (874) (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}''). The ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' records "Yabufuza Shrine, five seats (two myōjin-tai and three small seats)". In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, Suisenzan Fugen-ji served as the attached temple. In {{ill|lt=1814|Q6943|WD=Q6943|1=1814}} (Bunka 11), {{ill|lt=Ino Tadataka|Q1240937|WD=Q1240937|1=Inō Tadataka}} visited during his {{ill|lt=surveying|Q816425|WD=Q816425|1=Surveying}} expedition. === Precinct === File:Yabu-jinja, shaden-2.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Yabu-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall File:Yabu-jinja, tou.jpg|Stone hōkyōintō pagoda (Yabu City designated cultural property) File:Yabu-jinja, Yamanokuchi-jinja.jpg|Yamanokuchi Shrine File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Festivals === * {{ill|lt=February 25|Q2356|WD=Q2356|1=February 25}} Spring Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}) * Third Sunday of April and preceding day: {{ill|lt=Ohashiri-san|Q11263474|WD=Q11263474|lt=Oshiri Festival|1=Oshiri Festival}} - designated intangible folk cultural property of Yabu City * {{ill|lt=May 2|Q2545|WD=Q2545|1=May 2}} {{ill|lt=Hachijūhachiya|Q11390609|WD=Q11390609|1=Hachijūhachiya}} Festival * {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}}) === Cultural properties === === Registered cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture === * Tangible cultural properties ** Yabu Shrine, 5 structures (buildings) - registered March 5, 2010 (Heisei 22){{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/29kentouroku.pdf |title=List of prefecturally registered cultural properties |website=Hyogo Prefectural Education and Training Institute |accessdate=2020-01-13}}. *** Main hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Worship hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Yamanokuchi Shrine - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Gosha Shrine - built in the late Edo period. === Yabu City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Stone hōkyōintō pagoda - designated May 18, 1977 (Showa 52)Explanation board for the stone hōkyōintō pagoda.. * Intangible folk cultural properties ** Oshiri Festival === Access === * Rail / bus ** About 2 km on foot from {{ill|lt=Yabu Station|Q871175|WD=Q871175|1=Yabu Station}} on the JR {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}} ** From JR San'in Main Line Yabu Station, take {{ill|lt=Zentan Bus|Q11389110|WD=Q11389110|1=Zentan Bus}} bound for "Yōka Station" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" ** From JR San'in Main Line {{ill|lt=Yōka Station|Q870988|WD=Q870988|1=Yōka Station}}, take Zentan Bus bound for "Wadayama / Yamaguchi" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" * Roads ** About 1 hour from {{ill|lt=Fukuchiyama Interchange|Q11594317|WD=Q11594317|lt=Fukuchiyama IC|1=Fukuchiyama interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway|Q11613730|WD=Q11613730|1=Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway}} ** About 20 minutes from the Asago-side exit of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tōen Tunnel Toll Road|1=UNKNOWN}} ** About 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Wadayama Interchange|Q10921611|WD=Q10921611|lt=Wadayama IC|1=Wadayama interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Bantan Renraku Road|Q11498699|WD=Q11498699|1=Bantan Renraku Road}} ** About 5 minutes from {{ill|lt=Michi-no-Eki Yabu|Q11640978|WD=Q11640978|1=Roadside station Yabu}} and {{ill|lt=Michi-no-Eki Tajima Rakuza|Q11641146|WD=Q11641146|1=Roadside station Tajima-rakuza}} === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Ōyabu Kofun Group|Q83458374|WD=Q83458374|1=Oyabu Kofun Group}} * Hotaru no Sato * {{ill|lt=Manpuku-ji (Yabu)|Q43594125|WD=Q43594125|lt=Manpuku-ji|1=Manpuku-ji}} - a famous temple known as "Kōya of Tajima" === Notes === {{reflist}} === References == = * Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San'in'' (new reprint), 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 * ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 19 San'indō 2, Kogakkan University Press, 1984 * ''Hyōgo-ken Jinja-shi'', lower volume (reprint of 1938 edition published by Hyogo Shrine Priests' Association), Rinkawa Shoten, 1984, ISBN 4-653-01102-8 * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1175348 ''Tango Shiryo Sosho'' Vol. 4], Tango Shiryo Sosho Kankokai, 1927, National Diet Library Digital Collections (accessed June 17, 2025) ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Yabu-jinja}} * [http://www.yabu-jinja.jp/ Yabu Shrine] - official site * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/380201.html Yabufuza Shrine, Five Seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Yabu, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Yabu, Hyogo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Tajima Province]]

seat|WD=Q135018062|1=Engishiki seat}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+5', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県養父市養父市場840', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yabu-jinja}} [[JA:養父神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11666856}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yabu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yabu-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yabu|Q869014|WD=Q869014|1=Yabu, Hyōgo}} Ichiba 840, Yabu City |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|14.6|N|134|48|13.7|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}
{{ill|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Kimi|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}}
Funahotari-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-taisha|1=Myōjin Taisha}}: 2 seats, small: 3 seats)
{{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727|1=Tajima Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (tradition) 30th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} with {{ill|lt=hinoki bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事 = Oshiri Festival (third Sunday of April and the preceding day) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Yabu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yabu-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Precinct entrance}}]] '''Yabu Shrine''' (やぶじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ichiba, {{ill|lt=Yabu|Q869014|WD=Q869014|1=Yabu, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is the {{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727|1=Tajima Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=third-ranking provincial shrine (sannomiya)|1=Ichinomiya}}, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (2 myōjin-taisha seats and 3 small-shrine seats), and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is popularly called "Yabu no Myojin-san" and is known as a deity of agriculture. Ichiba in Yabu has long been a center of cattle markets for {{ill|lt=Tajima cattle|Q13417172|WD=Q13417172|1=Tajima cattle}}, and auctions are still held at nearby Ōyabu. === Enshrined deities === The five currently enshrined seats are: * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} * {{ill|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Kimi|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}} * Funahotari-no-Mikoto - descendant of Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and ancestor of the provincial rulers of Tajima In ''Tajima-kō'', a regional record compiled in the Edo period, it is described that once the upper shrine on Mt. Yadaka enshrined Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto, the middle shrine on the mid-slope enshrined Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto and Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine at the present site enshrined Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto. ''{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}'' treats four seats other than Ōanamuji as uncertain. ''Yabu Gunshi'' (1928) gives the upper shrine deities as {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}} and {{ill|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, the middle shrine as Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine as Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and Funahotari-no-Mikoto''Gods of Japan (7) San'in'', p.331. === History === Tradition says the shrine was founded in the 30th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and it is already named together with {{ill|lt=Izushi Shrine|Q3156511|WD=Q3156511|1=Izushi Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Awaga Shrine|Q11604651|WD=Q11604651|1=Awaga Shrine}} in the ''Tajima no Kuni Zeiseichō'' of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 9 ({{ill|lt=737|Q23583|WD=Q23583|1=AD 737}}). For divine court rank, it received junior fifth rank lower in {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 12 (845) (''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}''), rose to senior fifth rank lower in Jōgan 11 (869), and to senior fifth rank upper in Jōgan 16 (874) (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}''). The ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' records "Yabufuza Shrine, five seats (two myōjin-tai and three small seats)". In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, Suisenzan Fugen-ji served as the attached temple. In {{ill|lt=1814|Q6943|WD=Q6943|1=1814}} (Bunka 11), {{ill|lt=Ino Tadataka|Q1240937|WD=Q1240937|1=Inō Tadataka}} visited during his {{ill|lt=surveying|Q816425|WD=Q816425|1=Surveying}} expedition. === Precinct === File:Yabu-jinja, shaden-2.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Yabu-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall File:Yabu-jinja, tou.jpg|Stone hōkyōintō pagoda (Yabu City designated cultural property) File:Yabu-jinja, Yamanokuchi-jinja.jpg|Yamanokuchi Shrine File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Festivals === * {{ill|lt=February 25|Q2356|WD=Q2356|1=February 25}} Spring Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}) * Third Sunday of April and preceding day: {{ill|lt=Ohashiri-san|Q11263474|WD=Q11263474|lt=Oshiri Festival|1=Oshiri Festival}} - designated intangible folk cultural property of Yabu City * {{ill|lt=May 2|Q2545|WD=Q2545|1=May 2}} {{ill|lt=Hachijūhachiya|Q11390609|WD=Q11390609|1=Hachijūhachiya}} Festival * {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}}) === Cultural properties === === Registered cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture === * Tangible cultural properties ** Yabu Shrine, 5 structures (buildings) - registered March 5, 2010 (Heisei 22){{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/29kentouroku.pdf |title=List of prefecturally registered cultural properties |website=Hyogo Prefectural Education and Training Institute |accessdate=2020-01-13}}. *** Main hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Worship hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Yamanokuchi Shrine - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Gosha Shrine - built in the late Edo period. === Yabu City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Stone hōkyōintō pagoda - designated May 18, 1977 (Showa 52)Explanation board for the stone hōkyōintō pagoda.. * Intangible folk cultural properties ** Oshiri Festival === Access === * Rail / bus ** About 2 km on foot from {{ill|lt=Yabu Station|Q871175|WD=Q871175|1=Yabu Station}} on the JR {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}} ** From JR San'in Main Line Yabu Station, take {{ill|lt=Zentan Bus|Q11389110|WD=Q11389110|1=Zentan Bus}} bound for "Yōka Station" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" ** From JR San'in Main Line {{ill|lt=Yōka Station|Q870988|WD=Q870988|1=Yōka Station}}, take Zentan Bus bound for "Wadayama / Yamaguchi" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" * Roads ** About 1 hour from {{ill|lt=Fukuchiyama Interchange|Q11594317|WD=Q11594317|lt=Fukuchiyama IC|1=Fukuchiyama interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway|Q11613730|WD=Q11613730|1=Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway}} ** About 20 minutes from the Asago-side exit of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tōen Tunnel Toll Road|1=UNKNOWN}} ** About 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Wadayama Interchange|Q10921611|WD=Q10921611|lt=Wadayama IC|1=Wadayama interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Bantan Renraku Road|Q11498699|WD=Q11498699|1=Bantan Renraku Road}} ** About 5 minutes from {{ill|lt=Michi-no-Eki Yabu|Q11640978|WD=Q11640978|1=Roadside station Yabu}} and {{ill|lt=Michi-no-Eki Tajima Rakuza|Q11641146|WD=Q11641146|1=Roadside station Tajima-rakuza}} === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Ōyabu Kofun Group|Q83458374|WD=Q83458374|1=Oyabu Kofun Group}} * Hotaru no Sato * {{ill|lt=Manpuku-ji (Yabu)|Q43594125|WD=Q43594125|lt=Manpuku-ji|1=Manpuku-ji}} - a famous temple known as "Kōya of Tajima" === Notes === {{reflist}} === References == = * Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San'in'' (new reprint), 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 * ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 19 San'indō 2, Kogakkan University Press, 1984 * ''Hyōgo-ken Jinja-shi'', lower volume (reprint of 1938 edition published by Hyogo Shrine Priests' Association), Rinkawa Shoten, 1984, ISBN 4-653-01102-8 * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1175348 ''Tango Shiryo Sosho'' Vol. 4], Tango Shiryo Sosho Kankokai, 1927, National Diet Library Digital Collections (accessed June 17, 2025) ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Yabu-jinja}} * [http://www.yabu-jinja.jp/ Yabu Shrine] - official site * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/380201.html Yabufuza Shrine, Five Seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Yabu, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Yabu, Hyogo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Tajima Province]]

[[JA:養父神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11666856}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yabu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yabu-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yabu|Q869014|WD=Q869014|1=Yabu, Hyōgo}} Ichiba 840, Yabu City |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|14.6|N|134|48|13.7|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}
{{ill|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Kimi|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}}
Funahotari-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-taisha|1=Myōjin Taisha}}: 2 seats, small: 3 seats)
{{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727|1=Tajima Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (tradition) 30th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} with {{ill|lt=hinoki bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事 = Oshiri Festival (third Sunday of April and the preceding day) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Yabu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yabu-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Precinct entrance}}]] '''Yabu Shrine''' (やぶじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ichiba, {{ill|lt=Yabu|Q869014|WD=Q869014|1=Yabu, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is the {{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727|1=Tajima Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=third-ranking provincial shrine (sannomiya)|1=Ichinomiya}}, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (2 myōjin-taisha seats and 3 small-shrine seats), and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is popularly called "Yabu no Myojin-san" and is known as a deity of agriculture. Ichiba in Yabu has long been a center of cattle markets for {{ill|lt=Tajima cattle|Q13417172|WD=Q13417172|1=Tajima cattle}}, and auctions are still held at nearby Ōyabu. === Enshrined deities === The five currently enshrined seats are: * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} * {{ill|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Kimi|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}} * Funahotari-no-Mikoto - descendant of Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and ancestor of the provincial rulers of Tajima In ''Tajima-kō'', a regional record compiled in the Edo period, it is described that once the upper shrine on Mt. Yadaka enshrined Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto, the middle shrine on the mid-slope enshrined Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto and Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine at the present site enshrined Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto. ''{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}'' treats four seats other than Ōanamuji as uncertain. ''Yabu Gunshi'' (1928) gives the upper shrine deities as {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}} and {{ill|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, the middle shrine as Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine as Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and Funahotari-no-Mikoto''Gods of Japan (7) San'in'', p.331. === History === Tradition says the shrine was founded in the 30th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and it is already named together with {{ill|lt=Izushi Shrine|Q3156511|WD=Q3156511|1=Izushi Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Awaga Shrine|Q11604651|WD=Q11604651|1=Awaga Shrine}} in the ''Tajima no Kuni Zeiseichō'' of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 9 ({{ill|lt=737|Q23583|WD=Q23583|1=AD 737}}). For divine court rank, it received junior fifth rank lower in {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 12 (845) (''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}''), rose to senior fifth rank lower in Jōgan 11 (869), and to senior fifth rank upper in Jōgan 16 (874) (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}''). The ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' records "Yabufuza Shrine, five seats (two myōjin-tai and three small seats)". In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, Suisenzan Fugen-ji served as the attached temple. In {{ill|lt=1814|Q6943|WD=Q6943|1=1814}} (Bunka 11), {{ill|lt=Ino Tadataka|Q1240937|WD=Q1240937|1=Inō Tadataka}} visited during his {{ill|lt=surveying|Q816425|WD=Q816425|1=Surveying}} expedition. === Precinct === File:Yabu-jinja, shaden-2.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Yabu-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall File:Yabu-jinja, tou.jpg|Stone hōkyōintō pagoda (Yabu City designated cultural property) File:Yabu-jinja, Yamanokuchi-jinja.jpg|Yamanokuchi Shrine File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Festivals === * {{ill|lt=February 25|Q2356|WD=Q2356|1=February 25}} Spring Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}) * Third Sunday of April and preceding day: {{ill|lt=Ohashiri-san|Q11263474|WD=Q11263474|lt=Oshiri Festival|1=Oshiri Festival}} - designated intangible folk cultural property of Yabu City * {{ill|lt=May 2|Q2545|WD=Q2545|1=May 2}} {{ill|lt=Hachijūhachiya|Q11390609|WD=Q11390609|1=Hachijūhachiya}} Festival * {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}}) === Cultural properties === === Registered cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture === * Tangible cultural properties ** Yabu Shrine, 5 structures (buildings) - registered March 5, 2010 (Heisei 22){{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/29kentouroku.pdf |title=List of prefecturally registered cultural properties |website=Hyogo Prefectural Education and Training Institute |accessdate=2020-01-13}}. *** Main hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Worship hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Yamanokuchi Shrine - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Gosha Shrine - built in the late Edo period. === Yabu City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Stone hōkyōintō pagoda - designated May 18, 1977 (Showa 52)Explanation board for the stone hōkyōintō pagoda.. * Intangible folk cultural properties ** Oshiri Festival === Access === * Rail / bus ** About 2 km on foot from {{ill|lt=Yabu Station|Q871175|WD=Q871175|1=Yabu Station}} on the JR {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}} ** From JR San'in Main Line Yabu Station, take {{ill|lt=Zentan Bus|Q11389110|WD=Q11389110|1=Zentan Bus}} bound for "Yōka Station" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" ** From JR San'in Main Line {{ill|lt=Yōka Station|Q870988|WD=Q870988|1=Yōka Station}}, take Zentan Bus bound for "Wadayama / Yamaguchi" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" * Roads ** About 1 hour from {{ill|lt=Fukuchiyama Interchange|Q11594317|WD=Q11594317|lt=Fukuchiyama IC|1=Fukuchiyama interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway|Q11613730|WD=Q11613730|1=Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway}} ** About 20 minutes from the Asago-side exit of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tōen Tunnel Toll Road|1=UNKNOWN}} ** About 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Wadayama Interchange|Q10921611|WD=Q10921611|lt=Wadayama IC|1=Wadayama interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Bantan Renraku Road|Q11498699|WD=Q11498699|1=Bantan Renraku Road}} ** About 5 minutes from {{ill|lt=Michi-no-Eki Yabu|Q11640978|WD=Q11640978|1=Roadside station Yabu}} and {{ill|lt=Michi-no-Eki Tajima Rakuza|Q11641146|WD=Q11641146|1=Roadside station Tajima-rakuza}} === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Ōyabu Kofun Group|Q83458374|WD=Q83458374|1=Oyabu Kofun Group}} * Hotaru no Sato * {{ill|lt=Manpuku-ji (Yabu)|Q43594125|WD=Q43594125|lt=Manpuku-ji|1=Manpuku-ji}} - a famous temple known as "Kōya of Tajima" === Notes === {{reflist}} === References == = * Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San'in'' (new reprint), 2000, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 * ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 19 San'indō 2, Kogakkan University Press, 1984 * ''Hyōgo-ken Jinja-shi'', lower volume (reprint of 1938 edition published by Hyogo Shrine Priests' Association), Rinkawa Shoten, 1984, ISBN 4-653-01102-8 * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1175348 ''Tango Shiryo Sosho'' Vol. 4], Tango Shiryo Sosho Kankokai, 1927, National Diet Library Digital Collections (accessed June 17, 2025) ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Yabu-jinja}} * [http://www.yabu-jinja.jp/ Yabu Shrine] - official site * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/380201.html Yabufuza Shrine, Five Seats] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yabu, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Yabu, Hyogo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Tajima Province]]

[[JA:養父神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11666856}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Yabu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yabu-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yabu|Q869014|WD=Q869014|1=Yabu, Hyōgo}} Ichiba 840, Yabu City |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|14.6|N|134|48|13.7|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}
{{ill|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Kimi|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}}
Funahotari-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-taisha|1=Myōjin Taisha}}: 2 seats, small: 3 seats)
{{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727|1=Tajima Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (tradition) 30th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} with {{ill|lt=hinoki bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事 = Oshiri Festival (third Sunday of April and the preceding day) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Yabu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yabu-jinja, entrance.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Precinct entrance}}]] '''Yabu Shrine''' (やぶじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ichiba, {{ill|lt=Yabu|Q869014|WD=Q869014|1=Yabu, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is the {{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727|1=Tajima Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=third-ranking provincial shrine (sannomiya)|1=Ichinomiya}}, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (2 myōjin-taisha seats and 3 small-shrine seats), and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is popularly called "Yabu no Myojin-san" and is known as a deity of agriculture. Ichiba in Yabu has long been a center of cattle markets for {{ill|lt=Tajima cattle|Q13417172|WD=Q13417172|1=Tajima cattle}}, and auctions are still held at nearby Ōyabu. === Enshrined deities === The five currently enshrined seats are: * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} * {{ill|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Kimi|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto}} * Funahotari-no-Mikoto - descendant of Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and ancestor of the provincial rulers of Tajima In ''Tajima-kō'', a regional record compiled in the Edo period, it is described that once the upper shrine on Mt. Yadaka enshrined Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto, the middle shrine on the mid-slope enshrined Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto and Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine at the present site enshrined Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto. ''{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}'' treats four seats other than Ōanamuji as uncertain. ''Yabu Gunshi'' (1928) gives the upper shrine deities as {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}} and {{ill|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, the middle shrine as Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto, and the lower shrine as Tanba Michinushi-no-Mikoto and Funahotari-no-Mikoto''Gods of Japan (7) San'in'', p.331. === History === Tradition says the shrine was founded in the 30th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and it is already named together with {{ill|lt=Izushi Shrine|Q3156511|WD=Q3156511|1=Izushi Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Awaga Shrine|Q11604651|WD=Q11604651|1=Awaga Shrine}} in the ''Tajima no Kuni Zeiseichō'' of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 9 ({{ill|lt=737|Q23583|WD=Q23583|1=AD 737}}). For divine court rank, it received junior fifth rank lower in {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 12 (845) (''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}''), rose to senior fifth rank lower in Jōgan 11 (869), and to senior fifth rank upper in Jōgan 16 (874) (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}''). The ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' records "Yabufuza Shrine, five seats (two myōjin-tai and three small seats)". In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, Suisenzan Fugen-ji served as the attached temple. In {{ill|lt=1814|Q6943|WD=Q6943|1=1814}} (Bunka 11), {{ill|lt=Ino Tadataka|Q1240937|WD=Q1240937|1=Inō Tadataka}} visited during his {{ill|lt=surveying|Q816425|WD=Q816425|1=Surveying}} expedition. === Precinct === File:Yabu-jinja, shaden-2.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Yabu-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall File:Yabu-jinja, tou.jpg|Stone hōkyōintō pagoda (Yabu City designated cultural property) File:Yabu-jinja, Yamanokuchi-jinja.jpg|Yamanokuchi Shrine File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Subsidiary shrine in precinct File:Yabu-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Festivals === * {{ill|lt=February 25|Q2356|WD=Q2356|1=February 25}} Spring Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}) * Third Sunday of April and preceding day: {{ill|lt=Ohashiri-san|Q11263474|WD=Q11263474|lt=Oshiri Festival|1=Oshiri Festival}} - designated intangible folk cultural property of Yabu City * {{ill|lt=May 2|Q2545|WD=Q2545|1=May 2}} {{ill|lt=Hachijūhachiya|Q11390609|WD=Q11390609|1=Hachijūhachiya}} Festival * {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}}) === Cultural properties === === Registered cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture === * Tangible cultural properties ** Yabu Shrine, 5 structures (buildings) - registered March 5, 2010 (Heisei 22){{Cite web|和書|format=PDF |url=http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/29kentouroku.pdf |title=List of prefecturally registered cultural properties |website=Hyogo Prefectural Education and Training Institute |accessdate=2020-01-13}}. *** Main hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Worship hall - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Yamanokuchi Shrine - built in the late Edo period. *** Main hall of Sessha Gosha Shrine - built in the late Edo period. === Yabu City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Stone hōkyōintō pagoda - designated May 18, 1977 (Showa 52)Explanation board for the stone hōkyōintō pagoda.. * Intangible folk cultural properties ** Oshiri Festival === Access === * Rail / bus ** About 2 km on foot from {{ill|lt=Yabu Station|Q871175|WD=Q871175|1=Yabu Station}} on the JR {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}} ** From JR San'in Main Line Yabu Station, take {{ill|lt=Zentan Bus|Q11389110|WD=Q11389110|1=Zentan Bus}} bound for "Yōka Station" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" ** From JR San'in Main Line {{ill|lt=Yōka Station|Q870988|WD=Q870988|1=Yōka Station}}, take Zentan Bus bound for "Wadayama / Yamaguchi" and get off at "Yabu Myojin" * Roads ** About 1 hour from {{ill|lt=Fukuchiyama Interchange|Q11594317|WD=Q11594317|lt=Fukuchiyama IC|1=Fukuchiyama interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway|Q11613730|WD=Q11613730|1=Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway}} ** About 20 minutes from the Asago-side exit of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tōen Tunnel Toll Road|1=UNKNOWN}} ** About 15 minutes from {{ill|lt=Wadayama Interchange|Q10921611|WD=Q10921611|lt=Wadayama IC|1=Wadayama interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Bantan Renraku Road|Q11498699|WD=Q11498699|1=Bantan Renraku Road}} ** About 5 minutes from {{ill|lt=Michi-no-Eki Yabu|Q11640978|WD=Q11640978|1=Roadside station Yabu}} and {{ill|lt=Michi-no-Eki Tajima Rakuza|Q11641146|WD=Q11641146|1=Roadside station Tajima-rakuza}} === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Ōyabu Kofun Group|Q83458374|WD=Q83458374|1=Oyabu Kofun Group}} * Hotaru no Sato * {{ill|lt=Manpuku-ji (Yabu)|Q43594125|WD=Q43594125|lt=Manpuku-ji|1=Manpuku-ji}} -
